Representative_Docs: ['i understand the way llms operate well partly because i don t live under a rock but i have also spent a lot of time using it and i ve seen a lot of it in my courses as for use in the classroom the short answer is yes but i also know from my work that generic unedited ai writing consistently scores as very difficult for most people to read and comprehend based on fleshing kincaid gunning fog and every other established measure for the reading level of a given text this does worry me in just how accessible all these possible revolutionary uses for are most students reading and literary skills have already been in decline and i know that a lot of my students in comp i and ii at an open enrollment cc don t have the skills to be able to use ai meaningfully yet the thing that annoys the shit out of me about the white paper you linked and most current ai discussion on par with it is that it all more or less boils down to there are so many possibilities but none of the suggestions are very concrete or actually useful i use ai pretty much every day for various things but none of those things are any of the things the white paper suggests those things are all just here s a thing that you can do that you probably don t really need or want to do but chatgpt can do it even though you don t have any use for it we still haven t figured out the best ways for students or faculty to use it really here s the thing that i can t stand about articles like this though it is working on an assumption that all students are honest and care about the integrity of their work it implies that if we just teach them how to use ai responsibly instead of discouraging it and penalizing it then they will magically use it for all these amazing things here s my experience i adjunct as a side hustle at a school where we cannot penalize or discuss suspicions of ai use with student because the powers that be are operating on this same bullshit assumption of well we can t accuse students without proof and we can t stop them from doing it so let s just encourage critical thinking and honest learning it has been a complete policy failure ai use is fucking rampant easily of students are using ai for every discussion every reply every essay and they re not even trying to hide it they don t even realize that their discussion post is a regurgitated version of about seven other students posts they make for the most boring and tedious discussions but students are also using ai and posting things that are just so wildly unaware of what s going on i ve had students reply to my posts and try to explain basic writing concepts to me a writing professor because they can t even be bothered to read what i actually asked them in the post or what they re copy pasting out of chatgpt just as an example one of the discussion in the blueprint i teach asks them to evaluate one sample essay from a list of three options in their original psot and then reply to others this happened multiple times per class student a posts a generic ai generated evaluation of essay with vague comments full random writing buzzwords student b posts about essay and student a responds with i also evaluated essay i agree with xyz vague comment random writing buzzwords they don t even realize it s a different essay than theirs student c posts about essay and student a responds with i also evaluated essay i agree with xyz vague comment random writing buzzwords they don t notice that they ve now claimed to have evaluated a different essay in every reply with the same comments in every single one they don t even know what they re saying or what anybody else is saying and they re completely unconcerned with it so maybe i m jaded because of my side gig s complete mishandling of ai but i can only meet these support students don t be adversarial avoid punitive policies suggestions with skepticism we would be foolish to ignore that some students do not give a shit about the integrity of their learning some students just don t want to write a paper and they know they re not going to get in very much trouble with ai and all the ai literacy in the world isn t going to keep them from doing it some students only can be deterred with the possibility of a penalty in my opinion we re all just going to have to be in the trenches for several years before there will be anything that revolutionary to discuss and apply in terms of classroom application for incoming student as well as figure out the best practices for mitigating the integrity issues', 'hi i m not sure this is the right forum for these types of career questions but i thought it might be worth a shot to post here if there are any other subreddits forums that would be a better fit for me to post this in please let me know as a quick summary i m currently an entry level software engineer at a big tech company hoping to apply for graduate programs to study and do research in deep learning theory i m unsure about whether it makes more sense to apply for terminal master s programs versus ph d programs in computer science or potentially even applying for master s programs in math some background i recently graduated from a large state school in with a bachelor s in computer science my experience in undergrad had a pretty strong mix of theory focused cs classes advanced algorithms cryptography computational complexity etc with a few math courses differential equations modern algebra combinatorics i ve had a few research experiences in experimental machine learning some natural language processing some computer vision with published papers as a second author and one research experience in more theoretical computer science that didn t go super far after graduation i ended up acknowledge that this is a pretty expensive decision in both time and money tuition and or lost income from being a software engineer in the worst case scenario where things don t pan out i expect programs would make the most sense for me to apply to ideally i would prefer to join a ph d program in computer science applied math eventually but if i am not competitive right now i would also be interested in applying for a terminal master s program and hopefully get the chance to do additional research and strengthen my technical background before applying to ph d programs again i expect that my theory background is much weaker than typical graduate students working in these areas since when reading relevant papers i often notice topics like optimization functional analysis differential geometry random matrix theory etc that i haven t studied much at all formally also my past research experiences are much less focused on theory and much more focused on empirical deep learning research a few questions i have in my situation would it make sense to apply for master s programs in mathematics at a few programs as an alternative to applying for ph d programs in computer science directly as in does the route of master s in mathematics ph d in computer science make sense if i want to go into deep learning theory as a follow up how competitive do applications for a master s in math tend to be especially if i didn t major in math as an undergrad note i m not currently planning on applying for ph d programs in math since i expect my background and research experiences are woefully inadequate does it make sense to apply for master s programs in computer science i would expect that while i might have a more realistic shot at getting accepted in this than ph d programs in computer science or master s in math i might not end up getting as much opportunity to take the math courses that i would like to improve my math background i m also unsure about the overall value proposition of a master s in computer science since i already have a bachelor s and am considering getting a ph d which might result in a master s along the way are there master s ph d programs with a strong research focus that blends computer science and applied math i ve heard of the algorithms combinatorics and optimization programs at georgia tech and carnegie mellon which might be relevant for what i m looking for but i m wondering if there are other programs similar to these i m happy to provide additional information as needed', 'hi everyone sorry in advance for an incredibly long read but i really could use some advice and just want to make my situation clear i m going to be graduating college in about a week with a b s in computer science engineering with an a i specialization just a thing my college does that determines course requirements so i was required to take more ai related courses than say a student focused on game design and i m minoring in combinatorics to ordinary partial differential equations courses and after all of this only one company has ever responded to set up an interview they are a company that contracts developers out to other companies for around months then maybe the company you are contracted by will offer you a full time position maybe but they have little to no benefits and really low pay when compared to the average starting salary of cs students from my college but even with all that a bad job is better than no job to me at least i think but after having an initial informal video interview i haven t feel like i m probably just below average and will have to struggle so much harder to compete against all the other insanely talented people applying right alongside me so i really just want to know mainly look for experience over anything for recent grads did you have to stretch the truth about your cs skills on your resume say for example knowing a programming framework you have never used like spring in java or net in c am i just below average in terms of my abilities and really just any other advice or comments you have would be incredibly appreciated and thanks for reading this massive wall of text here is an image of my current resume obviously names have been changed for privacy']

Name: 0_chatgpt_gpt_chat_use
Representative_Docs: ['chatbots are pulling info from all available online sources many of these sources are biased or outright inaccurate when it comes to content instruction teachers still need to spend a lot of time combing through and verifying anything written by a bot especially for social studies history instruction primary sources are key learning tools a bot will fabricate a source but it won t find actual historic documents that are connected to an enduring understanding that one wants to teach there are so many parameters on scope and sequence that it would be hard to feed them all into a chatbot many times standards can be combined in one lesson some standards need multiple days to be taught some standards build on knowledge from prior years while others are new to students in a particular academic year so they need to be emphasized more to improve student recall a bot has no way to determine which standards build on other standards unless you spend time training it so it can t appropriately sequence them all of this comes before teachers make minor adjustments to fit the curriculum to fit the school year schedule i ve used chatgpt for some practice exercises but it s only a drafting tool for example when i taught main idea i could instruct it write a paragraph about dolphins on a fourth grade reading level the main idea should be in the first sentence i could then paste the paragraph into a worksheet delete the first sentence and have my students write the main idea sentence i also do a lot of grammar exercises with correcting fragments and run ons unfortunately a chatbot cannot write sentences that are deliberately incorrect i can have it generate correct sentences that i then introduce errors into that trims some writing time for me but it s no miracle before chatbots i used some automatic worksheet generators for math practice it s really hard to find ones that allow me to put the parameters i want on the practice exercises if for example i wanted mixed number subtraction with different denominators and regrouping it was faster and easier to make my own worksheet if you want to assess recall perhaps a chatbot can write some assessment questions it may be useful for generating rough drafts of questions however assessing higher order thinking skills and generating meaningful essay questions isn t something easily outsourced to a bot i ve used newsela with students it s essentially a bot that re writes news articles at different reading levels and then generates comprehension questions the questions it writes are garbage overly complex too detail focused and not aligned with grade level reading standards i still use the service but it requires my time to re write assessments', 'sophia ankel wed may am edt min read texas a amp m university commerce texas a m university commerce google streetview a professor at texas a m university used chatgpt to test whether they d used the software to write the papers chatgpt is not designed to detect material that it has previously composed itself a professor at texas a m failed more than half of his class after chatgpt falsely claimed it wrote their papers prompting the university to withhold their diplomas rolling stone reported on tuesday in an email sent to his class of seniors on monday dr jared mumm said that he had submitted three of their last essay assignments into openai s bot chat gpt to test whether any students used the software to write their papers i copy and paste your responses in this account and chat gtp will tell me if the program generated the content mumm who teaches agricultural sciences and natural resources wrote in the email misspelling chat gpt i put everyone s last three assignments through two separate times and if they were both claimed by chat gtp you received a he added as a result most of the seniors who had already graduated had their official diplomas put on hold by the university rolling stone reported mumm offered the class the chance to re do the assignment to avoid a failing grade however chatgpt was not designed to identify content created by artificial intelligence including its own there are several other programs including winston ai and content at scale that are able to do this the professor and texas a m university did not immediately respond to insider s request for comment the email was originally posted to reddit by someone called dearkick in a follow up post dearkick wrote that several students wrote to mumm to show him their timestamped google docs in an effort to prove that they did not use chatgpt however the professor ignored the emails instead only replying on their grading software in the remarks i don t grade ai bullshit dearkick wrote dearkick also said that one student had been exonerated and that mumm had apologized to them two other students had admitted to using chatgpt earlier in the semester which no doubt greatly complicates the situation for those who did not they added in a statement to pc magazine texas a m confirmed they were investigating the incident adding no students failed the class or were barred from graduating as a result of this issue the professor is working with the students to determine whether ai was used to write their assignments and if so at what level however the individual students diplomas will remain on hold until investigations are completed the use of ai technology in education has become a contested issue while some schools in texas have banned students from using chatgpt others are trying to incorporate the ai chatbot', 'if you want to make it harder to chatgpt than write making it to where they can t copy text from the exam and paste it may be a good option how are the articles delivered can you use something that chatgpt hasn t heard of and provide them a locked pdf that they can t highlight from to copy and paste i had a recent issue with my discussion forum with copy and pasting to chatgpt so i started typing the prompt in a document and posting a screenshot in the lms instead of text they can copy and paste it helped a whole lot if they have to type the shit manually into chatgpt it s a bigger bitch to do that than just write it i just copy paste the questions into the alt text so accessibility isn t an issue are you able to change the format of the exam i would consider moving away from multiple choice fill in the blank correct the word in a sentence matching put these things in order those sorts of things are trickier to use chatgpt for and get the right answer every time if you want to stick with multiple choice i would either split the two components of the exam and make them take each part separately and then i d give them a tight time limit with questions that appear one at a time or i would provide all the questions in a way they can t copy paste from locked pdf screenshot and then make the online exam basically an answer sheet where they just enter a letter for each one i teach literature online and for my exams are moving to video responses not sure whether that would work lastly i got my department to buy me a pro subscription to teachermade it s like a year or something and it has a setting that blocks them from pasting and they can t highlight from it either i don t know whether it would work in an exam but it s made ai pretty much a non issue for the homework and drafting work that i require it for the downside is there is no timer but you can set it to limited visibility in the lms maybe']

Name: 1_teachers_education_teaching_teacher
Representative_Docs: ['ai isn t replacing teachers it s replacing bad teaching the companies getting crushed they weren t adding enough value beyond what ai can do faster but great teachers ai can t touch engagement mentorship or emotional intelligence instead of fighting it education should use ai as a tool not a threat imagine ai handling the grunt work grading summarizing generating practice problems so teachers can focus on actual teaching sparking curiosity guiding discussions and giving students real world thinking skills students already rely on chatgpt cool now teach them how to question it how to fact check analyze and think critically because the future isn t about knowing answers it s about asking better questions tl dr ai won t replace teachers but teachers who use ai will replace those who don t', 'imagine a world where learning is as unique as each student where educators have superpowers to tailor lessons to individual needs and where administrative tasks take a back seat to creativity and engagement welcome to the realm of artificial intelligence ai in education a landscape where technology and learning converge to revolutionize the way we teach and learn the abcs of ai simplifying the complex at its heart ai is like a digital brain capable of mimicking human intelligence it s the wizard behind the scenes that powers recommendation algorithms on streaming platforms and helps self driving cars navigate complex routes but what s its role in education well ai offers educators insights that help tailor content predict learning outcomes and automate mundane tasks freeing up more time for meaningful interactions with students personalized learning the golden key remember the classroom where one size fits all ai is changing that with ai educators can understand each student s strengths and weaknesses creating customized learning paths that cater to individual needs imagine a math lesson that adapts its difficulty level as a student progresses or a language app that adjusts content based on the user s pace ai makes these scenarios not just possible but probable beyond the classroom the full potential of ai ai isn t just confined to student teacher interactions administrative tasks like grading attendance tracking and even scheduling can be automated giving educators more time to focus on impactful teaching strategies moreover ai powered chatbots provide instant assistance answering queries and guiding students through their learning journeys all while promoting engagement looking ahead a glimpse into the future the impact of ai on education is only beginning to unfold as technology evolves ai s potential will expand leading to innovations we can t even imagine yet the question is how can educators harness this potential how can institutions seamlessly integrate ai to enhance learning while addressing concerns about ethics and privacy as we navigate this exciting frontier let s explore together the intricacies of ai in education join me in discovering how ai is reshaping classrooms enabling educators to become facilitators of curiosity and giving students the tools they need to thrive in a tech driven world ready to dive in let s create together if you re excited about sharing the transformative power of ai in education with your audience let s collaborate together we can craft insightful articles that demystify ai and its impact on learning reach out to me and let s embark on an educational journey that ignites curiosity and empowers learners aiineducation edtech edtechstartups', 'here s what chatgpt has to say the impact of ai on education can be both positive and negative depending on how it is used positive impacts of ai in education personalized learning ai can be used to analyze the learning patterns of individual students and tailor education programs accordingly allowing for a more personalized approach to education increased efficiency ai can automate administrative tasks such as grading and record keeping freeing up time for teachers to focus on teaching and interacting with students improved accessibility ai can provide access to education for individuals who may not have access to traditional education due to physical disabilities or geographical barriers enhanced learning experience ai can provide interactive and immersive learning experiences through the use of virtual and augmented reality allowing students to engage with material in new and exciting ways negative impacts of ai in education loss of jobs ai may replace some human jobs such as grading and administrative tasks leading to job losses for some individuals overreliance on technology if ai is not used appropriately it may lead to overreliance on technology and a decrease in critical thinking and problem solving skills among students data privacy concerns the use of ai in education requires the collection of data which may raise concerns about privacy and the security of personal information overall the impact of ai on education depends on how it is used while there are potential negative impacts the positive impacts such as increased efficiency and personalized learning can greatly benefit both students and teachers it is important to use ai responsibly and ensure that it is used in ways that enhance the educational experience for all students']

all seems locked behind cs degrees or a master s and i m currently pursuing a statistics degree i m fully aware of the necessity of internships projects self learning and i m mostly concerned with data engineering software engineering tl dr question how would a bs statistics degree with lots of programming experience projects etc fare in the tech job market how important is the cs degree for Name: 3_student_evidence_academic_report
Representative_Docs: ['i just received my grade for my midterm assignment for the snhu m snhu exp success strat online c course which i am in my last days of the term and submitted my final paper yesterday but my instructor gave me an f and a zero on one assignment because she has accused me of violating my academic integrity policy by using artificial intelligence generating software to complete my assignment the allegations are without a doubt false yet after submitting my paper for review by the office of community standards they determined that i did violate the policy and i did use a i to write my paper but i didn t i submitted an appeal but the decision was not overturned i received a verbal warning and received no credit for the assignment which decreased my a grade to a c and now only if i get on my final can i increase my grade to s maximum of an b i think it is a travesty that without undeniably definitive firsthand eyewitness evidence of using a i there is no way to prove or even identify a difference between completely original text and a i generated text especially since the method of analyzing an assignment is an a i software that scans the documents and cross references the internet previously submitted assignments and literary works to compare similarities flagging the identified items i tested the software with my final paper that i took extra care guaranteeing that everything was written by me and it still determined of the paper was plagiarized it flagged everything from commonly used phrases the assignment s template text the questions the references the school name and frequently grouped words that are inherently relevant to and unavoidably necessary to the course material not to mention many items that are purposely mimicking the content of the assigned material designated as the information we are intended to learn so my grade and ultimately my collective gpa is being penalized because i retain the information i take in almost verbatim in addition to the similarities between my writing technique and style closely resembles the style that is used in textbooks and websites simply because it is the correct method to structure a paper it alsois a mathematical impossibility to produce entirely unique and original content when you are completing an assignment that uses a provided template that has been used repeatedly every term for every student that has ever enrolled in the course and expect that the correct responses and information that is expected to be regurgitated and be graded as accurate without being repeated at some point either on the web or in a previously submitted paper what should i do what can i do the course final grade is going to be submitted friday so they didn t give me very much time to try', 'yes another dreaded ai post i had a student submit a paper that immediately sounded like ai and was not reflective of class content nor their previous writing the bizarre language use also made me think they used a text spinner on top of this as some checks i ran it through an ai checker and got obviously not a perfect indicator but made me think this could indeed be ai i then ran my paper prompt through good old chatgpt which gave a few outputs that were extremely similar in both style and content to the student s paper including the nonsense stuff that didn t come up in class i compared to previous writing samples and it did not match i emailed the student and specifically laid out my concerns and what evidence i would need to prove this is their own work they showed up to the meeting with nothing claiming computer trouble would prevent them from showing previous work i asked them to explain various parts of the paper elaborate on examples and they could not do so i showed them the similarities between their submission and ai content and they were like huh that s weird i let them know that at this point i would need to submit an academic integrity report because they could not prove the work was theirs i said if they could produce evidence they try appealing i showed my chair and they agreed the evidence was compelling so i have at least one outside source suggesting i m not crazy the student keeps asking me what they need to do to appeal i ve clearly laid out the evidence they d need and on monday turning off the email notifications for the thanksgiving break will direct them to the academic integrity group to sort it out have you had a student like this before just persistently denying despite a mountain of evidence against them have any of them ended up being able to show satisfactory proof that the work was theirs the student has not been combative but is clearly frazzled which makes me feel badly it s just seems so unlikely this is their work it s so frustrating to have to deal with this', 'greetings all like so many of you i just wrapped up the fall term at my institution and i just need some colleagues to give me some perspective on what is real right now for this term i updated my syllabi to include language dedicated to ai misuse after getting caught flatfooted in the spring by not having such clear policies listed i stated a three tier policy on plagiarism academic dishonesty and our writing standard apa with a first level being a warning generally when students just made a mistake with a citation etc a second level being a zero for an assignment generally when the same students continued to make the same citation errors or when there was a more egregious issue of plagiarism occurring and a third level being a class failure for when students repeatedly plagiarized or used ai at a rate higher than on turnitin s detector along the way i caught students through the detection system and of them confessed to using it when presented with the evidence two others said they were unaware that using grammarly to essentially write their papers was considered a violation and another just decided to fight everything despite very obvious evidence on multiple assignments my institution responded by having the vp issue a statement that essentially told us to stop using ai detection as a means of supplying evidence in order to balance the needs of academic integrity with fairness towards students this letter seemed to run counter to our state s laws which clearly state all grade penalties are to be assessed by the faculty member as noted below the class instructor is responsible for handling each case of academic dishonesty in the classroom and for determining a penalty grade as outlined in the course syllabus if within the instructor s professional judgement reasonable evidence would suggest a student engaged in academic dishonesty the instructor will provide notice to the student either written or verbal of their assertion of academic dishonesty and of the academic penalty grade within thirty instructional days of the occurrence or when the instructor is made aware of the occurrence the instructor will submit a report of the assertion of academic dishonesty the explanation of the notice or actual notice given to the student and a copy of all applicable evidence to the student conduct officer sco at this time the instructor can request that the incident only be documented with the sco unofficially or they can officially refer the matter for disciplinary action if the student has a previous academic dishonesty record then the sco can choose to move forward with the disciplinary process without an official referral the faculty union drafted a letter asking for the vp s letter be rescinded based upon this language to no avail since that point in time finals have occurred and students seem far more emboldened to cheat while it feels increasingly more like our hands are tied on a personal level this is taking a massive toll on me mentally and emotionally it s hard to stay as engaged and involved in my class discussions student projects and maintain my optimistic and positive outlook on my program and my school when i feel repeatedly unsupported and rejected by the institution i m losing sleep feeling a sense of dread and am becoming so frustrated with all of this i recognize that i m not alone in this and this is hardly a unique to me circumstance but i also have a really difficult time when others essentially won t hold the line with me and seem to be consistently undercutting me for taking a principled stand on academic integrity i m now on break and i just want to completely disconnect at the same time i ve never felt that way before in my years of teaching i ve always taken my evaluations and final results and immediately gotten excited about tweaks to make to my classes for the next term i ve always had a positive outlook on academia and felt a sense of belonging even when i previously lost jobs due to budget cuts while on the tenure track now i feel isolated alone and unsteady i don t know what to do am i going crazy or do i simply just care too much should i just let it go and let the proverbial criminals run free do i double down on this fight what do i do what do we as faculty do thanks to anyone who can give me some moral support sound judgment or profound wisdom at this time']

Name: 4_ai generated_generated_written_detectors

Name: 5_writing_students_write_ai
Representative_Docs: ['this question depends entirely on what you think about writing why you assign writing what you value in a student s writing what you want them to know about writing when they move beyond your course etc why did you need to write a dissertation what purpose did it serve for the institution issuing your degree i can tell you this i do not assign writing because i have a shortage of things to read i teach composition so i assign writing to see how well students are learning to write for me put simply expository writing of the category i teach is the process of making your thinking accessible to someone else packed into thinking includes research synthesis broad brush strokes but i think you can extrapolate the extent i think ai writing is legitimate if you consider churning out boilerplate responses for customers you never want to meet legitimate as one other commenter suggests then you should have no problem with students churning out ai generated text but why read their papers if you re going to have students use ai to generate text and if you re not going to read them why have them write them for you and for the above it all trailblazers who think all of this is no big deal because you have a product oriented view of writing when you ask your students toy around with ai as part of their assigned writing in your course i d greatly appreciate it if you made clear that by assigning them to generate text that way you are not normalizing it and they should not assume that s okay for their papers in other courses', 'i teach both creative writing and rhet comp and i have dumped all my write in the style of prompts from my curriculum for the reasons you stated it s difficult to be in the teaching of writing business right now if we try to force students to turn their back on ai completely as if we could we appear as luddites if we embrace it too fully we remove the analytical rigor from our courses for my classes we will not use ai during class but i will encourage its limited use outside of class after i model its ethical use for my and level courses most of my graded assignments will be far shorter than before much of the graded work will be started and finished in class using pen and paper i will grade each step of the process and i will give a lot of credit for effort in comp we will use ai to help choose topics then fine tune them in class using pen and paper cue the tears but tough when we have a solid thesis set we will go to ai for outline ideas they will try several prompts and see how the different responses work or do not work they will print the best outline and bring it to class then we ll begin revising and start our writing process again in class and using pen and paper students will do research outside of class they ll bring that research to class and write an annotated bibliography with pen and paper next they will practice incorporating evidence into support paragraphs again with no electronic support once i have seen they understand how to do the analog critical thinking parts of the writing process i ll unleash them to complete their essays on their laptops and submit a longer form essay for cw the homework will be the dreaming ideation process they will bring ideas to class and write out basic structures on paper they ll revise those structures during class and complete them for the next class on their laptop their final version will need to maintain the basic structure they set during the previous class i realize this will stifle some of the creative juices and limit some but far from all of the inspiration outside of class but it s the only fair way for the honest students to be on a level playing field with those who will be tempted to use ai ai plagiarism checkers are unreliable and our ability to detect ai work by simply reading the essay becomes harder every day i d argue by next month it will be impossible to declare conclusively an essay is ai generated and the students know this again this is for lower level classes and i will of course make allowance for those students with accommodations my seniors won t need this kind of encouragement and by now i know their writing well enough i think to distinguish it from ai at least i will tell them that', 'if i were teaching writing courses i d have students parts of two class periods to write and revise drafts on a simple topic i d think the writing samples you obtained from those two sessions would be enough to determine the ability of the students in a class and you could inform them that you were using it as a baseline to compare their later work against a few more propositions are the following first frame for your students what you consider to be acceptable use of an ai tool for aid writing their assignments i personally would consider it fair for them to use the bot to help with brainstorming topics and copy editing their work i would not consider it acceptable to use a program to generate de novo text that the students then revised themselves the main point i d be getting at is forcing them to submit work where the contents of the work are their ideas to elaborate on the previous point i strongly suggest offering examples of how to use ai effectively as a writing aid and how not to the points i shared above are what i believe to be best unmentioned was topic summarization if you want to convince students that ai cannot write their papers from scratch demonstrate that to them one way to do this is to use an ai to generate an entire essay on a topic then critique it and show them why the ai models aren t adequate for the task show them what the models are good for and where they fail convince them through evidence that they can t just use models to do their work for them second require students to submit first drafts of their papers as well as statements on how they used any ai to help with the assignment as part of that submission you could ask for a brief reflection on how the technology was helpful be very clear that the use of ai must follow guidelines you set out both in a syllabus and in lectures as in the example above violations of this policy whether stated in the reflection or in the actual work presented should be treated as violations of any relevant student codes of conduct or academic dishonesty and punished accordingly third assign essays that emphasize creative writing or elaborate argumentation in the process explain why one should bother writing at all i remember my writing classes requiring me to write argumentative and persuasive essays and to my surprise today i when i read review articles they are often written either as persuasive or argumentative essays some are written only to be informative reviews maybe as aggregated second drafts on an emergent topic but often authors taking the time to write a review paper are doing so to persuade their readers of something about the topic two examples i am fond of from biomedical engineering and statistics are fourth if you have students who aspire to become writers in the future i am sure this is a minority of students point out to them that most people are not that interested in reading novels written by ai models evidence for this claim is modern chess computers are leagues ahead of humans in chess and other games but computer tournaments receive practically zero viewership when compared with machines we still see the world chess championship played by two human players not by the top two chess engines available in a given year sure the humans are inspired by computer games and use computers to study but at some point the games become human versus human with all the flaws and ingenuity of human players a student may argue that art contradicts this point but i d argue that is only because ai generated art is novel in time we will prefer human creativity in all aspects to that of our would be overlords in super intelligent ai music literature art and competitive games would lose their luster if we knew an ai produced them there will be places for the ai but they will not replace humans outright last these models are created by companies their free availability now and into the future is not guaranteed students should be confident in their abilities to work without these models by their side even if there is a high probability that they ll never have to work without them again a lot of companies and academic labs are prohibiting their staff from using these models to write authentic work the people i know in industry cannot use it and i know of one high profile academic lab that also prohibits their use for writing a main point to get across is that any person working professionally must know how to write in some capacity i entered university in assuming this was one of the core expectations of any college graduate and it kept me awake as a freshman and later as a senior when i learned and relearned that i sucked at the task ai cannot pick a set of facts to write about any topic with it cannot always select a paragraph or essay outline that is best for conveying a set of facts or to deliver prose and citations students must learn these things for themselves they must learn to appreciate the maxim that clear writing means clear thinking and aspire to reach such lofty goals they must believe that they are capable of achieving this as well to close with one final anecdote and thank you if you ve read this far i am graduate student in statistics the worst experience grading i ve ever had was trying to understand a student s work when he or she could not form proper sentences in his or her native language in such cases i stressed to them that approximately correct computations were not enough that they had to be able to tell a story about the data and the modeling decisions made they needed to understand that the order facts arrived in could not be permuted at their leisure for example they could not tell me that a model was statistically significant or try to interpret model parameters before telling me what the model was in the first place in regression models they could not interpret model coefficients which are correlative as measuring a causal relationship unless they could justify the causality']

things that could have been avoided through structure and backbone i m not saying don t do the rants vents they re cathartic shouting out into the internet can help your mental health sometimes no judgement here i do it all the time i came into academia after years in industry and was pretty shocked with the changes or at least my perceived changes now that i was far removed and on the other side i almost left teaching after my first year out of frustration but stuck it out then covid happened and well i don t have to tell you how that all went and how it still is reverberating i have taken lessons from the situation and worked to keep my standards while building in a bit more of equity for students to help them get back on track when i say structure i mean your class setup and polices students need structure most of them are not learning how to learn or being taught how to college not giving them that of course a lot of them are going to fail and you may think that s not my job and to you i say bs it s in your best interest and the students best interest for you to address it and honestly it doesn t take a whole lot more effort or whole cloth rewrite of your course nor does it take away your standards in fact it helps students meet them and makes your life easier every line in my syllabus and instructions comes out a student not meeting or misinterpreting my expectations or just general college expectations you have to have an attendance policy that s number one they signed up for this class at this time the expectation is they be there you re not doing them any favors by not having them and thinking it s only about the things you assess for when a student is in class they benefit from things ranging from being introduced to concepts being able to ask questions even if most don t some do and all hear the answer seeing good students do things to model off of hearing you riff on your subject other thoughts getting immediate feedback doing a guided activity etc and communicate those reasons to the students even if some students aren t fully paying attention there s the osmosis factor i still give a number of free absences which is of my class periods because life happens but i also have an upper limit where they automatically fail the course of class unless there s something like hospitalization in the middle i reduce their final grade logarithmically full disclosure my biggest course only has students with most of my other classes being student workshops i make the tas have students sign in to the class a lot don t even know how to take notes consider skeleton notes it s a very equitable way to help them take notes some can t hear or see as well some are a little slower on listening or writing it also cues a student who doesn t take notes and pay attention to do it and they perceive it as easier as they don t have to frantically write or figure out how to best structure their notes don t mistake your capacity and ability to take notes as true for everyone i require they turn them in i don t grade based on accuracy just a checkmark of points if from feet they look fine and have all the pages probably the only other really required thing is a late work policy i take off as soon as it s past due and then each additional hours i don t accept work later then a week past due that cuts off any end of the semester bs i make no exceptions other than hospitalization another equitable thing i do is make all things due right at the beginning of a class none of this midnight bs some students actually have terrible internet you re not paying for it and some places have terrible internet by making it due then they can get to class a couple minutes early and upload to the system if there s some real issue with the lms you are there to verify it if they didn t come in and there was an issue you just hit them with the late penalty it s on them i actually had a student complain to me once about having it due before the class which blew my mind but i told him it s a due date not a do date and shared why i do it i do offer or hour extensions for any exercise in my classes that students can use no questions asked but they have to shoot me an email telling me they re using it i generally don t start grading within that time period anyway so it s no skin off my apple and that way they know they only have the or i don t get begging emails and it makes me look like a star because i build it right in and let them know that i realize they have bosses that don t coordinate i also track these extensions in the lms as their own item so they can see if they have any left once they re out they re out i d say about half of any given class uses them and it causes me no issue but i never give those extensions for larger multi week projects they have to time manage in that case but what i like to do on these projects is have concrete items that have to be done at various due times or they lose points i don t want students banging out the whole thing in one night i break major projects into deliberate smaller deliverables such as research exploration ideation drafts i make checklists of what those deliverables entail this by the way is how i think more classes will have to work as things like chatgpt become standard i also try to build in a peer group critique session if i can that way students get insight into how others are approaching i structure those as well by making them use the rubric and some guiding questions as they talk about each others work which brings me to rubrics i never once had a rubric in college when i got here students demanded them and now i love them it takes as much voodoo out as possible and speeds up grading it communicates exactly what and to what level i am looking for of various skills they have cons too like what i call checkmarking by students where they try to hit those items but somehow the piece as a whole doesn t hold together but i now have some qualitative items in it to account for that but let s face it grade inflation is what it is i will also say your context means you have to think about what your real expectations can be i teach all upper class students in my major i ll admit that i ll tell my students that if push comes to shove it s my class they should put more time and effort in than their gen eds i m sure that will bristle some of you but what i m teaching in a professional program honestly will help their specific goals more if they can do both well by all means they should but i call a spade a spade i also harp on the fact that grades don t really matter in the real world focus on learning obviously that is a bit lost on a lot of them if i taught gen ed my expectations would be pretty different not that gen eds aren t important but let s face it they re less important on the whole to the student than their major classes stick to a few objectives like writing and critical thinking or how your subject actually relates to their real world and not just knowledge for the sake of knowledge but accept that most of them are not there because they love the subject and are in any way invested i floated through a lot of my gen eds and i bet a lot of you did too i literally can barely remember any gen eds or my gen ed professors nor have i used any specific rote items other than the odd jeopardy question what i have used are skills like learning thinking argument writing etc i know a lot of the rants are about those very skills that s why you need to clearly communicate to the students that that is a real goal of the class don t assume they know it it might actually make them appreciate it more share more about those strategies and a little less about the topic to get them on your page not much it can be as simple as minutes at the end of a class discussing strategies for what you re asking them to do for assignments or how to study or whatever not the instructions but the meta concepts they won t remember any of your subject unless you draw a clear line to how it affects their actual lives besides getting a grade to pass i also point out constantly how much time any given course should take outside of class a credit class expects you to work hours outside of class according to my university i remind them when i assign something if a student doesn t do the work i remind them again if someone complains they spend so much time on it i ask them how much time some think a half hour or hour is so much time i will say seems like a lot and really hours each class and going to the classes is a hour week i know some have jobs and things but it is what it is they chose to be here and their goal is to learn not just pass through and get a degree for paying for their tuition they have to carve out the time i think a bit too many in modern college think it s for socialization and the classes are more an inconvenience from that you have to tell them they are mistaken yes that matters but that can t be the focus work hard play hard is fine but you can t just play hard and forget that first part students having to work is a real confounding factor that i feel bad for and part of the systemic issues of our society but i can t solve everything and sometimes life sucks before it gets better as far as backbone i m talking about yours you are not there to be their friends a friend will back up your buddy in a bar fight even though they were the one being the a hole you are there to be their teacher and mentor a lot of times that means telling them they re being an a hole obviously you have to say that in a nice way and also teach them how to not be that way but you have to be fine playing the heavy you re a full adult they are not they are still on the runway to adulthood some less far than others some will hate you for it that s kind of your job i even get a little tickle of excitement when i pass a student in the hallway that i know hates my guts just because i asked them to grow you have to stick to your policies if you build in that structure and some of that equity i talked about earlier it s pretty easy to then just look them in they eye and say that s the policy i can t change it for you or i d have to change it for everyone and you say that to students you both like and don t like no one is above the policies there will still be jerk students who will trash you on rmp and course evals oh well i ve actually stopped reading both unless admin calls me in about eval scores i assume i m fine i like to tell people i went into teaching because of the movie dead poet s society but when i rewatched it i realized that not everyone stands on their desk in the end you can t reach everyone you can t be liked by everyone plus you know life isn t a movie no one is standing on their desk in the first place most will not remember you you probably won t remember most of them you might not even be more than a blip in their life and vice versa i also see a few students take all your emotional energy and up until i came to this realization of all i ve said i thought that too but these students aren t tying you down and making you feel you are in control of your emotional energy i used to brood for the rest of the day when i had a bad interaction with a student or work came in and it wasn t up to my expectations now that i have the structure i feel is both fair to hold my standards and fair to be equitable to students because i really am there to help them i don t let that happen i shrug it off because i know i m in the right if they are being entitled or selfish that s not on me i have no problem looking a student dead in the eye and just telling them listen i know it s not what you want to hear but that s the way it is because of my policies which i believe are more than fair this matter is closed as far as i m concerned and showing them the door have the memory of a goldfish about these matters i know it s easier said than done but put that same emotional energy into the good students and interactions and a good student doesn t mean a good grade student but someone who is engaged and trying and yes some students might try to make some argument about wording in a policy but i just tell them this isn t constitutional law it s my intention that matters not their interpretation they could have clarified before taking any action that s on them it would have to be egregiously ambiguous language for me to concede but the more i ve dealt with things the more revision i have and i use the same policies in all my classes to keep it consistent so that s not likely to happen most of the times its because they misremember what you said and didn t bother consulting the written instructions or policy about accommodations i feel like they are being handed out like candy i fully admit that despite my own mental health issues i don t put the same respect to ones that are probably from that rather than physical issues which i guess is good why they don t tell you what they re for to keep my bias in check not that they still don t annoy me at least at my school the accommodations are less requirements as suggestions i tell students that my policies are already built with some equity for people who don t even know they can get accommodations so they aren t getting double whatever is being asked such as absences and late work a couple more at most and i hold the line at absences as a fail without hospitalization i already provide all my slides so that s no issue for me and if they need notes i tell them my slides are my notes which they are if they need note taking remember that skeleton notes thing you can ask another student to share with them anything i feel is unreasonable i have no problem telling them it won t work for me hasn t happened much some students think they get carte blanche or are retroactive i tell them they are mistaken we also have a contract we are supposed to fill out with a student when they share their accommodations that way it s detailed in writing and signed by both of us i suggest you do the same lastly students may like to say well professor x let s me do or something about past stuff they used to get away with and to that i say well gas used to cost a gallon until biden raised the prices just kidding i don t say that i m a socialist liberal who acknowledges the complicated global economy but i tell them that it doesn t matter as that was a different context and different expectations i m not professor x first of all i can walk is that x men joke ablest and the two have nothing to do with the other that s not how life works requirements can be different in different contexts i don t want this to come off holier than thou or for anyone to think i think i m a paragon of teaching i know i m not and i am still constantly learning and evolving maybe my context is different than yours but this is where i m at after teaching for close to a decade surviving the pandemic and feeling like i ve never been happier with or better at teaching it was a bumpy road to get here and i still have periods of sadness and frustration but i thought it might help some of you obviously you re free to not take any of this advice or add more advice below']

Name: 19_jobs_market_software_companies
Representative_Docs: ['why are people so optimistic about the job market improving the last decade was an anomaly that we will likely never see again interest rates were incredibly low unlike now and it does not seem like they will drop any time soon many tech companies are not even profitable and it is hard to see any disruptors in this space since cloud computing ai can change this but i do not see any evidence of that for now software will always be in demand but you do not need tens of thousands of engineers when a select group can achieve the same task twitter fired more than half of their workforce and weirdly enough twitter has been coming out with more features than ever after looking at this graph you cannot convince me that the market of before from before covid is coming back anytime soon lastly the it took about twenty years since the last set of layoffs in tech the dotcom bubble the job market will probably back to normal at some point but this might take longer than usual possibly a decade the era of job hopping and high salaries might be over too x b tldr the job market might take years or even decades to recover keep this in your plans in terms of your career', 'for one obviously no one can predict the future i have a friend at faang who is convinced ai will destroy engineering salaries he s worked with lots of smart people over the years i ve worked mostly in consulting agency work and i realize that business stakeholders usually don t understand what they want and lot of application are spaghetti so i m in the camp that ai will make engineering jobs more efficient your opinion of the direction of the market may also expose something about your personality that s a long side discussion anyway we ve had boogeymen in this industry before years ago they said that all the jobs would be offshored there was offshoring but it didn t work out how everyone wanted so there s been a shift back now there are two boogeymen ai and remote work ai is obvious people think ai will start churning out code and devs will no longer be necessary with remote work people think this will lead to more offshoring we re facing an economic recession we have high inflation that the government is trying to fight they re fighting this by raising interest rates the assumption is that eventually they will lower them again and this will help companies want to grow more aggressively that s the general assumption of how things will play out something i heard on the radio the other day is that one person thought ai reminded them a lot of the dot com bust back then you had companies slapping com to their names and it would help their stocks valuations we re seeing similar things were companies are trying to focus on ai perhaps it will be a game changer i m assuming more of a something that has influence and causes some disruption and then we get back to normal anyway we don t really know my guess is this field will continue to offer a pretty good lifestyle even if you aren t in a top tier company even if we move to a scenario with more ai generated code we ll need people to audit the code yes chances are in a lot of cases the quality will be fine but when lives and money are at stake you want to hope there will still be some type of process in place in the mean time you ll have people who are denial about change like me and then you ll have people talking doom and gloom the reality will probably be somewhere in the middle look at it this way when i was entering college years ago the job i do now didn t even exist you can t completely prepare for the future just try to put yourself in a good position to be able to respond and plan for when things change', 'this sub has become very anti ai doom so you likely won t get a good response from most i ll try to offer my cents we need to look at time horizons in will ai be a threat to developer jobs no the bigger threat is the overall state of the economy and how flooded the market is with highly compensated highly competitive engineers as interest rates have risen companies no longer feel safe to borrow money to pay expenses developer salaries cloud costs etc so r d will be cut down in which will likely mean an increase in layoffs and hiring phases my company just layed off of its software engineers across all skill levels architects senior mid devs and entry level solely because they are storing capital to weather what they believe will be a rough sadly this is the pandemic shutdowns finally catching up to the market just keep in mind that one of the goals of the fed raising interest rates is not only to stave off inflation but to also bring competiviness back into the markets e g lowering salaries by driving up unemployment neat huh part of their plan is to fuck you over to save the companies in the next years ai may develop the capacity to be a threat to software development careers but the world s adoption of ai and learning how to use it effectively will be lagging behind so software development positions will likely start to alter in functionality at this point developers may become less technically proficient as they become more reliant on ai to do an understand the code however the caveat is that there will still be a need for gifted engineers who can design systems and further continue to improve on ai models in the next years yeah i m not optimistic i think today s notion of a software developer will essentially morph into a combined product owner developer role because ai will be good enough that you won t really have to understand a ton of what is going on underneath the hood there will still be people who do understand it but the need for those people will be diminished outside of the tech sector this will be a contraction of classic software developer jobs in other industries particularly with those industries that don t have complicated problems to solve']

Name: 20_turnitin_turnitin ai_detection_plagiarism
Representative_Docs: ['sure why not believe them they re not saying very much of course so the stakes are pretty low consider for instance what they already say about what originality does they say that it investigates the authenticity of student work nice words what does that mean well here s what they say does turnitin detect plagiarism similarity does not equal plagiarism the two concepts are not the same there i said it this might ruffle some feathers and challenge some notions but we need to clear the air i ll say it again similarity does not equal plagiarism the answer however is simple turnitin does not detect plagiarism in an effort to clear up any remaining doubt let s go through some of the most frequent questions we hear one by one and try to answer each one as simply directly and clearly as possible the post goes on to repeat this refrain over and over turnitin does not detect plagiarism so through the magic of marketing turnitin is best in class plagiarism detection that explicitly does not detect plagiarism hooray further the turnitin originality suite is not just instructor facing it also has a student coaching product draft coach that helps students see similarity reports while they write so what turnitin is doing right now with their plagiarism suite is building a total wrap around product to sell to everyone so that it can basically engage in an arms race with itself chegg is in the process of closing the loop on this too probably some readers here have gotten blasted over the last few years by a marketing escalation from chegg for uversity sell us stuff to help students cheat and now now honorshield serve your exams through us and we ll block your students from looking at our site on the student facing side they also offer students an essay builder with built in citation checkers and plagiarism detectors here too they want to sell tools that facilitate cheating and tools that supposedly combat it anyway all of this is to say that we should absolutely believe that all of these ed services companies are going to be scrambling to build ai writers and tools to detect ai writers in the hopes of capturing all the dollars they possibly can it strikes me as sort of unbelievable that some institution is going to just opt out of their turnitin subscription unless they re about to jump on board with a different plagiarism detector like grammarly for education they re all competing for the same space and i doubt any institution can afford not to subscribe to one of them since as turnitin s marketing reminds them their institution s reputation is at stake', 'the turnitin ai detector makes clear that it s essentially a beta product and cannot make any conclusions nor can any conclusions be drawn from the probability metric it presents the turnitin ai detector gives a probability of there being ai work in the paper not a match of the prose like the turnitin plagiarism detector it sounds like your professors didn t even go thru the instructor tutorial on turnitin i bet you could probably use turnitin s own statements about the tool to make your case my university has prohibited instructors from using their ai detector tool as evidence for now their plagiarism detector is pretty spectacular at matching papers to those already published online or catching students submitting each other s work from prior terms', 'what i have found is that the turnitin engine is good at red flagging assignments quickly when i then read the assignments i am able to determine quickly that yes the flagged submission is ai generated i determine this by looking for evidence of ai hallucination such as arguments that do not originate from the primary sources quotes that are not representative of anything stated by the authors and so on there is also some characteristic language in the submissions that backs up my own assessments the benefit of using turnitin s ai detection is primarily as an adjunct to your own expertise and awareness but also because students are used to getting caught by turnitin i have had a number of students argue with me in the past that their work isn t plagiarized because turnitin didn t catch it but i can clearly show them that it is a line by line paraphrase of a source it s the same with this if i see problems i respond accordingly for comparative purposes i have found that i can generate text using chatgpt that is not flagged by the other two detectors but which is flagged by turnitin however modifications to the text can make detection more difficult just as it does with plagiarism thus my position is that i am the one teaching the course not turnitin and ai detection is just one of my tools']
