Name: 25_key_output_used_lesson
Representative_Docs: ['extremely helpful had it generate a reading about democracy and tyranny with word counts at the end to track reading fluency in a history class to make it interesting i told it to run that reading through again but this time use north korea and the united states as the examples of democracy versus tyranny had it create a list of key factoids over the difference between the spartan oligarchy and athenian democracy with emphasis on the rights and responsibilities of slaves children women and men in greek society anytime kids do reading notes i have it generate a grade level appropriate text on the topic then i edit and append in key information from the textbook and key information you know they ll never put in a fuckin textbook generate a list of ideas for collaborative movement based bellwork i could talk forever about chatgpt if you create alot of your own shit and don t use ai to assist you these days you re wasting alot of time you can honestly have a generate questions about a text that you already have if you want i don t think i ll ever do that though because my questions and analysis in class are very directed towards a certain target i want them to hit however if you want it could create everything for you with no actual effort from yourself i would say not to do that because the beauty of ai is it saves time but it does not replace the human part of pedagogy', 'ok well maybe i am the outlier here it has brought about a small renaissance in my household i use it to generate reading comprehension tests at specified levels in my kid s interest generate passages about topics that i want to teach my kid about while also weaving in the things he is interested in such as minecraft roblox and classic godzilla movies and quizzes based off of this bounce ideas off of i asked it for instance if the workload my kid was taking on was likely too intensive for his age it said yes and then i went back and forth with how to rework it to improve this gain ideas from when we re starting a new topic i ask it what everyone should know about the topic usually i ask it for things everyone should know so for instance for computer literacy it said block based coding i didn t know what that was it gave me some examples and we started incorporating that into our day my kid loves it build activities that teach specific concepts using specific tools for instance i d like to do a chemistry lesson that uses minecraft please write an activity appropriate to a year old child to accomplish this generate a kind of cliff notes of important concepts that i want him to get from books without him actually having to read those books and while relating it to or weaving in the topics that he is interested about i ve also perhaps controversially been explicitly teaching him to use it and told him i have zero qualms with him using it to generate emails messages essays to proofread to debug code or to gain information though with the caveat of needing references if the information is important i do proofread and ensure everything looks good before we roll it out because it isn t foolproof and sometimes it gets a little lost and i have to start a new chat it ll pull quiz questions from multiple passages instead of just one for instance it isn t a perfect tool but it has absolutely completely changed the landscape of how we are learning i think the fear around it gives me very don t use google no reputable scientist has ever used google to find things do you think doctors use google to look things up you should only ever use the encyclopedia on hold in the library that is the one and only reputable source of knowledge vibes if kids don t know how to use this in years it will be the equivalent of a full grown adult saying they don t know how to use the internet as a side note here i have heard that the microsoft edge version of chatgpt is significantly better at generating sources for its content that are accurate and reliable but i have not yet had the ability to try this out another aside i m a computer science student and i m also doing my second degree right now and the ai detectors can t really detect ai great example got posted on here of it spitting out that the constitution was ai generated they re capitalizing on the fear around it just like there was fear around using wikipedia and google and the internet and books there was fear that women who read fiction would be unable to tell it from fact and bicycles and tvs they re selling a product that doesn t really work to grab money from schools who are knee jerk reacting rather than sitting down and considering how to work with a disruptive new technology', 'i use it as a sounding board most of the time something that talks back if you will but yes on daily use it becomes apparent that in a sanitized environment with clear instructions it can do some seemingly amazing things however in the real world it s not nearly as powerful as it seems i was in one of my debugging sessions and asked it something along the lines of how can i get this function to output this right now it s output is this it gave a perfectly reasonably sounding improvement as well as explained what the code was doing except that the improvement it suggested was literally regurgitating my original function back with no changes another time i asked give me a set of input and output for caesar of hello world from key to key format each input and output in the form of an array here is the output it gave input hello world output for key ifmmp xpsme output for key jgnnq yqtnf output for key khoor zrugo output for key lipps asvhp output for key mjqqt btwiq output for key nkrrv cuxjr output for key olssw dvyks output for key pmttx ewzlt output for key qnuuy fxamu output for key rovvz gybnv output for key spwwa hzcow output for key tqxxb iadpx output for key uryyc jbeqy output for key vszzd kcfrz output for key wtaae ldgsa output for key xubbf mehtb output for key yvccg nfiuc output for key zwddh ogjvd output for key axeei phkwe output for key byffj qilxf output for key czggk rjmyg output for key dahhl sknzh output for key ebiim tloai output for key fcjjn umpbj output for key gdkko vnqck output for key helpl wordl in the above output every key from to is wrong what clued me in was that as we all know at key it should revert back to the original string so i asked this as a follow up give me the same but with key and it gave me this gem sure here s the input and output for the caesar cipher of hello world with a key of input hello world output for key hello world note that the key value modulo is used to determine the actual shift applied in the caesar cipher in this case a key value of is equivalent to a key value of so the output is the same as with key this was my first experience realizing that while it can retain some context it doesn t seem to have knowledge of what it said before if you know what i mean it wasn t until i asked why is the output of key the original string hello world then that it corrected itself my personal opinion is that it s great as a reasoning aid just don t let it do the thinking for you or trust anything it gives out completely it is great for stock data or boilerplate code for example when building a page and i need lorem ipsum text i just ask chatgpt to generate text in the context of the page i am working on makes it easier to see how it might look at the end']

Name: 26_code_code base_tests_write code
Representative_Docs: ['one technical note while current models chatgpt codex can write code in general the longer the code they try to write the more likely they make a mistake that s actually exactly the same as humans by the way most experienced programmers could write lines of code without mistakes but trying to write lines of code means it s inevitable you ll make mistakes you ll have to find and fix anyway most programmers work on teams building large software with hundreds of thousands of lines of code or more i ve worked on software that had million lines of code before ai isn t anywhere close to being able to understand all that', 'that s great but i want to see it function in the real world i work in erp and one of our main financial batch job transitions involved hundreds of ai translated routines at first i was excited but now i can safely say i m not impressed and that ai is not even close to replacing half an employee let alone be better than of the staff on that is a good number to say how much the system tried to rewrite and code in a rather crazy half accurate half drunk manner it did a decent job with schema and mundane work but had a terrible time using native java routines rather than rebuilding everything from scratch also of the code ran absolutely all of the hundreds of jobs we sent to the vendor needed at least an hour per job of involved development and fixing and this includes simple etl processes ai is such a mixed bag and management is so eager to use it without understanding it at this stage it is indeed truly hype and this article isn t helping soon they will realize they can t depend on ai even for of the translated code let alone and they ll realize it won t build good maintainable code without a bonafide developer and that s the bottom line it can build quick fast crap code for one off transitions that don t need to be maintained and yes that is where it wins but if we want quality maintainable code that needs to be constantly refactored and updated then you must build it with an engineer who needs to handhold the process from start to finish and even then it s better and faster for the developer to just do it from scratch so no i m not at all worried about a new ai system that can code better on hackerank esque challenges than of the contestants even if it s not like that and is building a fancy app chances are the answers are there in opensource and is not a big deal for anyone savvy to find only thing that will impress me is if gemini is able to migrate one erp system from one language to another cobol to java flawlessly and doesn t involve rewriting a simple math round function crazy callbacks and weird variable references when it can do that i ll be looking for a new job but by then i probably would have learned the ins and outs of ai so again not worried', 'the majority of people will never be able to describe business rules with enough clarity to get an ai to magically produce software even the most talented programmer with decades experience will struggle with it because the best way to describe code is to write it so the best ai generated code will be based on code a programmer is already writing it s just more code completion if there was an easier way to describe algorithms and business rules then gui based code less app builders would have caught on years ago they end up being useful up to a point then someone that actually understands how to write code needs to fill in the blanks which ends up being more work than just writing the code']

Name: 27_jobs_labor_people_wfh
Representative_Docs: ['it s a good question the thing with ai is it poses very little threat to people who have to do serious analysis it really isn t intelligent at all but is good at recognizing patterns reshuffling and repeating it s a randomizer which is also not random fascinating and useful but not intelligent unless one defines intelligence as rote learning which computers have been able to do anyways for a lone time people doing code regurgitation people doing formulaic non analytic content writing etc will be impacted by ai but we need actual innovative coders and actual innovative writers mediocre ones just phoning it in will be threatened and as the pipeline for workers feeds through these lower levels there may be serious issues in the future unless we explicitly promote and provide good experiences in truly innovative approaches yet it is like the difference between people pushing a car and people riding in a car one everyone does something even if not really that productive or useful the other still needs a driver and still needs a mechanic but the rest are freed think about the human computers of old good at preforming lengthy mathematical calculations who were replaced by simple calculators mathematician s didn t disappear just the human computer same thing with ai and every other job this creates the interesting thing that although this won t happen unless governments force it to we could in theory reduce working hours economists have argued this for over a century now but ai could force governments to respond also calling what we have now ai threatens to draw attention and resources away from the really groundbreaking and actually intelligent ai projects into this warped conception of what ai is thereby restricting what we expect of actual ai and reducing innovation', 'the standards that you are held to are different than the standards ceos are held to and no that wasn t an ad hominem that was a statement of fact you are admitting to things that most wfh employees keep to themselves out of fear their employers will hear that s not a judgment that s an observation because that s also what the data is showing you need to shut the fuck up and stop using that phrase i can find data to support my claims too you clearly haven t done research before this is my ad hominem not the above you can t anthropomorphize data as your omniscient confirmation bias manifest and yes me and like was it i d have to double check of the population who now put wfh above medical benefits on the list of importance when applying to new positions now show me how many jobs provide wfh versus how many jobs provide medical benefits also that s a stupid decision medical benefits obviously matter more than working from home why the hell would i want to sit in a cubicle with you knuckle dragging morons for most of my life you re intolerable and undisciplined so you re at a disadvantage for in office jobs not to mention the savings in gas professional clothing lunch and most days breakfast and dinner because it s easier car maintenance the carbon reduction wfh is immense as well as clearing up traffic finally one good point no one wants to return to the office pretty much true it s over no it s not because people still want jobs and companies still don t exclusively offer wfh it would have taken them less than a year to realize this the companies that realize this will have the best talent and those highly sought after x devs those that cling to the old ways will go the way of ibm and sega but don t take my word for it we will both see in years who was right we would have seen over the past three years do you not think that three years has been enough time to witness wfh and to measure its effectiveness the ai boom took the internet by storm and wfh is just another attribute of a job not taking the job market by storm if it were truly the holy grail of efficiency as you make it out to be everybody and their mother would be wfh and companies would be rolling in money for doing so instead it isn t as cut and dried as you claim', 'my general problem with this stance is that it takes a relatively narrow view of the impacts of increased labor productivity on the amount of jobs available which is sort of the essence of the lump of labor fallacy this is applicable broadly across the economy as well as within individual industries it s easy to look at massive technological advancements and deduce that the same work could be accomplished for cheaper via fewer workers needed to produce the same output and in a vacuum this is obviously true however it doesn t address how increases in productivity force industries and individual businesses within them to adapt specifically increases in worker productivity means that from the perspective of firms the cost of labor is cheaper decreases in the cost of labor subsequently lowers barriers to entry for competitors to enter a business s market and forces them to adjust their pricing to compete with new market entrants existing companies expanding into their market or new companies altogether and or differentiate their products services enough to justify their pricing within the increasingly competitive market this all results in lower prices and better products services being provided to consumers meaning both b c and b b consumers with their increased purchasing power form demand in new areas or increase their demand in an existing areas and then businesses move into those markets to capitalize on that consumer demand this is a cyclic process and happens all the time this is obviously a foot view of how the economy adjusts to increases in labor productivity and does not address the rate at which these changes occur which i think is an important discussion to be having it s also important to note that certain markets still have enormous moats that make it less penetrable by competitors in the face of lower costs of production regardless though i disagree with the common sentiment around labor market doom from forecasting how new technologies and increases in labor productivity applied within the existing context of a company or industry will lower the amount of labor available without specifically addressing why the output of firms in a market or total number of firms in a market can both remain static it s completely valid to acknowledge that new technologies will have a huge impact on the scope of jobs going forward it s completely valid to have concerns about the rate at which jobs might change relative to the rate that firms will be forced to adjust especially since transitory periods of unemployment can have significant consequences for individuals with all of this said even the extremely bullish outlooks on the advancement of ai ignore the other side of the coin lower costs of production mean you either lower prices due to more firms being able to offer a competitive product service or you produce more both of which push labor demand upwards and incentivize firms to mobilize workers to the areas of the highest roi i personally think it s far easier to envision a future where more businesses exist and they are either maximizing their differentiation or broadening their participation into more markets in order to compensate for new competition driving prices down in individual markets and in that future i think individual workers would just be responsible for a lot more']

Name: 28_reviewers_journals_journal_review
Representative_Docs: ['not all ieee trans are equal if you are looking for machine learning papers ieee tpami is the gold standard the other transactions especially the topic specific ones like power systems might be lower quality it s normal to submit to a specific application based journal if your method isn t novel enough for a ml specific journal it s also normal for experts of the specific field but beginners to ml to submit to the application based journals if you want higher quality ml methods look in tpami the ml specific ieee transactions editors accept reject papers based on the reviews they may or may not actually read the paper each paper has about reviewers if problems aren t caught by one of the three reviewers it won t be caught now consider that reviewers are busy people they probably don t spend more than a few hours reviewing a paper a paper might get lucky and have lenient reviewers it s like rolling dice all peer reviewed journals conferences are like this at least in machine learning i didn t read the papers you are referring to but there are a lot of papers that use neural networks for nonparametric regression nonparametric doesn t mean that there are no parameters it just means that the model is defined by the data rather than learned by the data if that makes sense', 'hi all i recently stumbled upon a concept called post publication peer review pppr regarding academic paper publishing in scientific journals the basic idea is that your paper is published as soon as you submit it and peer review follows after that which tend to be more transparent than traditional pre publication peer review i find it funny that this never crossed my mind when i was doing my phd and struggling getting published nevertheless i can see a few pros and cons of this concept cons dissemination of info without validation comes with risks of premature unjustified results conclusions we have seen that in with covid papers and now with ai preprints but preprint servers are now very common to use for certain fields the prestige of getting published in a high impact journal becomes less meaningful since the barrier is lower e g only editorial screen traditional journals and established researchers would be opposed to the idea of pppr i think people might be reluctant to submit to a pppr journal for possibly having their basic mistakes exposed to the scientific community a prepublication review prevents that pros lower the barrier and shorten the time to getting published this potentially helps more junior researchers potentially but not necessarily more transparent peer review i e both authors and reviewers are public this can also help reduce the bad or ill intentioned reviews one might get reviewers can get the credit for their critical work in the process of science publishing possibly ease the monopoly of big publishers springer elsevier sage etc and the high fees that they impose on us the researchers overall i am inclined to think pppr would create a better environment for open science but i am not sure i have found a few examples of pppr out there like pubpeer among others i am curious if anyone has any experience using something like that and why we would or would not move towards a more pppr style scientific publishing and away from the traditional version what do you folks think', 'first it needs to be said computer science cs is a large discipline with various subcultures the more close you are to mathematics and computational modelling the more you are likely to publish journals list authors in alphabetical order etc even within ai the way you will write a paper for the formal symbolic community is different than in the machine learning ml speaking about ai the field moves fast is part of the reason indeed that people focus on conferences we move fast due to the nature of the discipline not only our applied industry led research works a bit different than other applied disciplines e g pharmacology we due to the nature of the work benefit a lot from industry improvements in computational resources in fact the latest ai boom started due to the availability of cheap and powerful gpus for gp gpu computing rather than major methodological improvements due to the nature of the experiments if you are not in human robot interaction or high end robotics if someone else has the same idea as you they can beat you to it particularly in ml hence the need to claim ownership through arxiv as soo was possible moreover the structure of the papers is different as typical background related work seldom cite publications more than years ago this reduces the amount of space needed having worked with multidisciplinary coauthors i would say that journals in philosophy and psychology are actually much faster than cs they also allow for multi stage revisions something that we lack this might be due to a vicious circle people are busy reviewing for conferences journals submissions are processed slower people publish more at conferences there are of course other reasons there is a strong culture for open access which conferences tend to provide even in our journals we have been pushing for community led oa journals since the s there is also a culture for international collaborations and having in person discussions this is also why we have dagstuhl shonan aaai spring symposia etc in addition to normal conferences and workshops x b honestly i think they can fly too fast and loose it is not that straight forward as i said even within ai let alone cs as a whole there are multiple subcultures for example formal reasoning people from mas typically found at aamas ijcai kr etc tend to be overly critical of every mathematical proof and discussion while ml researchers focus more on the evaluation if you submit to a conference like neurips ijcai etc that attract submissions from multiple communities not all of your reviewers may be expert or care for the same things this is a side effect of having an ai summer and most conferences getting record number submissions every year right now given the ai summer most conferences are overrun by sota submissions and have reduced their typical reviewers to just each reviewer then has to do papers and meta reviewers way much more than that due to the sheer number and fast turnover some papers may fall from the cracks and there is an element of randomness in acceptance the biggest problem is that we do not have a second round of reviews any revisions are done on an honour system the community in theory self regulates who ignores and who doesn t feedback having said that my psychology and sociology coauthors always complain that cs reviewers are too harsh minor complains and you get a borderline score or major revisions if you ever review at cogsci you will notice this trend this is an unfortunate side effect of being used to a need get it right on the first try if you plan to publish at a top end venue']

Name: 29_lesson_teachers_lessons_educators
Representative_Docs: ['i went to your link i saw a lesson on how to write a cv i would say based on what i ve seen your tool has almost zero educational value beatrice just sat there and talked at me imagine a teacher standing in front of a room just talking at their students that would be an absolutely horrendous class i actually had a class like that once it was a chinese history class at nus where the prof just sat at the front of the room and read from a book it was an absolutely worthless class the worst class i ve ever had i stopped going and just read the book myself and this is what you have created with beatrice an example of the worst class i ve ever taken i think well designed computer teachers have the opportunity to revolutionize education but what you have created isn t worth anything here are the steps to creating a great computer teacher have humans create the lessons there should be or lessons on each small topic for example lessons on converting a parabola from standard form to vertex form each of the lessons should use different techniques have different teachers some of them actual humans some of them avatars have different styles the job of the ai is to pick which of these lessons the students should be given it knows the past performance of the student it knows the performance of all the students it has ever had so it can figure out which previous students are most like the current student and it can look at which lesson worked best for those previous students and make an educated guess on which lesson will work best for the current student think of it like netflix recommendations except it is picking out lessons instead of movies and it is maximizing future performance of the student the ai should not create the lessons ai sucks at that still the student needs continous and varied interaction with the computer teacher your cv lesson given by your ai will hold the attention of the average student for about seconds the computer teacher needs to ask the student questions and get back answers there should be many different ways to get feedback from the student and the feedback should be evaluated to figure out how well the student is understanding and figure out what the next lesson should be there should be direct interaction between students they should have group projects discuss problems come up with solutions more advanced students should be assigned to explain topics to less advanced students of course this will take some work but it will have value your ai teacher also took much less work but it has no value just a couple more details about my interaction with beatrice i asked it if it was going to just talk at me or if it would show me sample c v s it said it would show me sample c v s it didn t show a sample c v so then i asked it to show a sample c v it gave me a website to go to i went to the website and it gave a page not found error you have a lot of work to do and you don t even seem to be going in the right directions talking heads are terrible teachers lectures are a terrible way to teach talking heads are an even worse way to teach sorry for writing a book but i d really like to see some good computer teacher projects but all i keep seeing is worthless', 'hey r edtech already cleared this post with a mod so hopefully it doesn t run afoul of any rules we re former educators years teaching math physics and running educational outreach at the hs college level turned ai researchers ex alphabet we ve been building an ai tutoring chat agent to help both students and educators parents and we were wondering if some of our fellow redditors here could play around with our demo and give us some honest feedback and discussion we built everything custom and did not use chatgpt in any way to train our ai model since we don t believe the current big models are very capable of being good teachers versus just telling users the answers since we re just two random people working out of our garage things are still very rough especially in terms of accuracy although the model should become more accurate as we improve data compute but we are optimistic that our from scratch model already seems more tutor like than other ai bots you can access the demo here it s free and we don t collect any info other than what s typed into the chat window any chance the folks here could let us know what you think we d like to understand what you like and don t like and how we can better serve learners and educators moving forwards also happy to discuss in general how people think ai tech can Name: 31_ethical_art_unethical_ethics
Representative_Docs: ['unfortunately the images that the ai art technology is using to create these images are pulling bits and pieces from other artists work to create them they may be so different people don t recognize the work or may be similar enough that people do it depends on how well the job is done it s a huge ethical discussion in the art community right now of if this is ethical creating copyright issues unlike text where the ai can stream together it s own sentences by analyzing other writing and it s different enough it can t fully create its own images it just pulls pieces from preexisting images with alteration to create its own many artists are finding that their names style are being imputed into the ai generators so people can make images in the style of a particular artist there s even been instances where artists signatures have appeared in the end result as a warped messed up piece that also got clipped by the generator the images show as original but the method of creation is anything but', 'hire a lawyer you re selling a product that is built upon the intellectual property of third parties without their consent just because that data is published out in the open doesn t mean you are free to use it any way you like especially if you plan to profit from it the site you re scraping it from may themselves not even have the legal rights to grant you such permission they might licence it from somewhere else legally it s an absolute minefield you could easily be sued if it s found out what you re doing that s if app stores even let you publish it in the first place i can t image this meets their strict guidelines ai developers at least have the excuse that what they re doing is transformative the work isn t being republished it s being used to train a program to produce its own original output that s no different from how humans learn or so they claim as far as i know it hasn t actually been tested in court it s unlikely you would be able to use the same legal defence that s just the legal and ethical issues on the technical side scraping is always a pain in the ass websites often change their layouts occasionally full redesigns put technical barriers in place like request limits or ip bans change their terms of use change urls or domains shut down entirely all things that might intentionally or incidentally jeopardise your revenue stream depending on if you re scraping just one off or if it s an ongoing thing from a business standpoint it s unwise to run a business that s entirely dependent on a third party supplier that you don t have any commercial contract with that s why i struggle to empathise with people whose livelihoods depend on some random social media platform like ok you built a business that operates exclusively from some facebook page and now your account mistakenly got suspended and you can t make money well that s a shame you should tell meta to fulfil their end of the supplier contract or face financial penalties oh what s that you don t actually have any contract well that was fucking stupid wasn t it', 'i have developed this statement for the ethical use of ai in a course that i will begin teaching on friday any thoughts you have on it would be appreciated it seems that ai is irreversible and instead of telling students that they cannot use it they need to be taught how to use it responsibly this is my best attempt to achieve that goal the ethical use of artificial intelligence as artificial intelligence becomes more prevalent in our daily lives it is crucial for students to understand the ethical considerations surrounding its use in academic settings in this course students are expected to use ai in a manner which upholds the values of academic integrity in general the use of ai should never compromise development in critical thinking research and analysis along that line the use of ai to generate content manipulate the tone of work create data or summarize shorten an author s work for quicker consumption is considered academic dishonesty and strictly prohibited alternatively students are encouraged towards using ai to assist in research and writing process in ways which encourage the learning process and mitigate menial tasks for example students may use ai to create a table to help visualize complex data develop personalized learning and reading plans and assist in correcting grammar and spelling errors please note that any unethical use of ai in this course will be considered academic dishonesty and may result in disciplinary action students are responsible for ensuring that their use of ai is in compliance with the course policies on academic integrity']

Name: 37_blockchain_crypto_hype_ar
Representative_Docs: ['even at the peak of blockchain hype the number of blockchain jobs was still small compared to the rest of the industry i expect ai will be similar until the hype dies down and if there s any merit to it then those bits will make their way into mainstream practice blockchain had no such merit that i can see but past hype waves have as a counterexample when mobile was new era it was overhyped too and everyone and their brother wanted a mobile app when the hype died down of course we found there was a lot of merit behind the hype but we also realized not everything needs an app because people don t want to install an app for everything which way will the current ai hype go who knows and if you find these things interesting then go ahead and learn about them but don t get the idea that the whole industry turns on a dime every time there s a new hype cycle they represent a small fraction of all the work out there', 'for the lazy someone put a link to an article about him the ai founder taking credit for stable diffusion s success has a history of exaggeration it s in his best interest to drum up the field and his company i ve only read the first part of the article so far but it sounds like someone stealing credit trying to make himself a star in the field and in a few years might be yet another person exposed as a fraud companies are panicked about being left behind so everyone is jumping onto ai i heard someone one the radio the other day say that the ai craze has a lot of similarities to the dot com rush where having dot com added to your company name increased valuations i think a lot of really great things will come out of some of these new developments but there will also be a lot of people taking advantage of and defrauding others just like we we with a lot of other big tech trends especially when they get mainstream attention we talked to amazon and said this will be the big thing mostaque told forbes from his bustling london headquarters in january they cut us an incredibly attractive deal certain personal guarantees and other things which i don t particularly want to go into because she ll be angry at me he explained nodding to zehra qureshi his wife and stability s then head of pr qureshi declined to elaborate but bratin saha a vice president for the seattle tech giant s ai arm told forbes in january that stability is accessing aws infrastructure no different than what our other customers do three former stability employees said that prior to its venture capital injection amazon had threatened to revoke the company s access to some of its gpus because it had racked up millions in bills that had gone unpaid for months asked for clarification stability conceded that the incredibly attractive deal mostaque had claimed was actually the standard discount amazon offers to anybody who makes a long term commitment to lease computing power any payment issues were managed in an orderly and communicative way with support from aws bishara said aws did not respond to multiple requests for additional comment here s an example years ago i worked with some type of content feed tool who claimed to have integrations with thousands of platforms they basically provided their data content in xml that was the extent and then it was up to you to ingest the xml', 'think about it this way assuming you have a basic understanding of what ai ml is and its potential what types of applications do you see for these new tools how many different areas of life do you see it improving things or making things more efficient ask the same question for ar then ask yourself how many of those new innovations could society just take her leave and how many of those would be quickly adopted as game changers in my opinion ai ml is going to have massive implications on our life ar will still be a niche item but i don t see it taking over anytime quickly i just can t think of things that i could do with ar that i can t do now that i really really want to be able to do that s absolutely not the case with ai and ml as soon as i saw chat gpt at this potential i automatically thought of thousands of jobs that i could automate at my old employer every time you see the implications of these models and when you tie them together with other technologies it s maddening the kind of effect this could have incidentally i did the same thing with comparing ai to crypto which is why i ve never been overly eager to get into the crypto industry crypto evangelist would keep coming up with these ideas on how to use either cryptocurrency or blockchain technology but i always found myself going back to the idea that it really didn t do that much of a better job for most people than what was already available if you were some super libertarian then using crypto versus fiat currency was very appealing however if you were just a regular person in a first world country i didn t see the benefit i could at least understand how it might be useful in developing countries dealing with hyperinflation but then i saw the barrier to widespread adoption']

Name: 39_calculators_calculator_math_use calculator
Representative_Docs: ['i find this very similar to artists who were upset about the new ais that can make art from just a prompt they are now embracing these tools as part of their workflow to create a first draft what sort of artists do you know the few artists i ve meet with those ideas are artists like i am an olympic swimmer i e not on the other hand many artists i have the pleasure of knowing see ai art as not a tool at all but a threat to the livelihoods of small artists trying to sustain themselves a threat to make society value art less and a threat to further commodify art as something corporatized if art love relationships and curiosity is what makes lift worth living why would we want to relegate those tasks to machines do we really care purely about mindless consumption enough that we are willing to dump some of the greatest outlets for what made humans so great evolutionarily creativity back on topic spellcheck is a tool sentence writing programming isn t a tool but simply cheating the reason why calculators are permitted in math is because the point of math isn t to routinely demonstrate your ability to preform lengthy calculations subjects which are writing centric however are of a different nature before calculators we had human computers often poorly treated ignored and underpaid people usually women who did the calculations for many major innovations but why could the math geniuses farm out this activity whilst still claiming to be a genius because they were the one with the ideas writing centric subjects are like that and it is through writing that you demonstrate your ideas since we think through language they are designed demonstrate your thinking whilst building your skills at writing skills unlike the ability to crunch massive formulas plenty of math crunching child geniuses turn out to be pretty bad at arriving at original ideas which then create a positive feedback loop of learning and growth plenty of those human computers had ideas too and probably good ideas but they were never explored lost or ignored regardless the point still stands and ai as it presently is is more like machine learning on a large scale than any actual art give a child a paintbrush and a canvas and they will do something with it give a computer that and it won t do anything and when it does it only mixes and mashes various ideas together that have been tossed into it that s not creation or originality but replication and randomness', 'first of all as per rule do not engage in or advocate for anything illegal on our subreddit this includes but is not limited to drug usage improper use medication prescriptions cheating plagiarism essay writing services copyright violation etc i am not advocating anything about these services nor am i mentioning any in particular i implore the sub s moderators to allow reasonable discussion under this post because this really is a conversation that needs to be had ok so a post was recently removed from this sub talking about these kinds of tools and i feel like there should be more of a discussion around these tools proffessors need to be aware that they exist and students need to know what the place of these tools is some people s gut reaction is that they should be excluded completely and that opening one of these websites during the school year is cheating but honestly that is just short sighted i m not saying this is the same at least not yet but think about the place that calculators used to have vs where they are now while the presence of calculators varies a lot between classes they are almost universally if grudgingly accepted in most circumstances on the other hand even glancing at a phone during an exam is an immediate trip to the dean s office in a lot of colleges so the question is is ai a new calculator or is it a smartphone personally i m gonna take a different stance i think most people are going to initially but hear me out obviously no you should not use it to write any portion of your paper for you aside from the fact that it would probably just get a lot of things wrong it is plagiarism even the most advanced tools get stuff wrong constantly however i have found it in excellent tool for brainstorming and rewording text that i wrote i think it has a valid role in the same way that example papers and tools like grammarly fit in grammarly will offer to replace words rephrase sentences and a great many other things but some professors even link to grammarly in their syllabus and i have heard using grammar checkers referred to as expected in many cases now obviously this is itself a nuanced topic because in a grammar class a grammar checker is probably frowned upon banned this is part of why i want to have this discussion with all the students and teachers in this sub for example if i have a paragraph that is too long i could feed that paragraph into an ai chat tool and ask it to shorten it it will probably make some mistakes and get things wrong but i could use the ideas it had about where to shorten it to do it myself if i gave it a then sentence long paragraph and told it this is too long shorten it and it gave me sentences back i could look at what it did and say it merged a few and removed some others i could then use that idea and remove similar information and merge some of the same others but all in my own style this is where a lot of the nuance comes in though because where you draw the line on what is inspiration and what is plagiarism is very difficult and varies a lot by case to sum up i would say it s use should be limited to breaking writer s block or remixing your own work to be clear do not include any text that comes from an ai tool but i would say it s probably fine and helpful to use it as inspiration get ideas from it not words as far as university policy i doubt any institutional response will show up for another year or two so in the meantime i would say teachers ought to include specific text in their syllabus about ai tools and what place the teacher thinks they should play i would encourage all you assignment writers not to include a blanket ban because at the end of the day these tools will be used but right now you have a short window of time to influence how', 'agreed simple calculators are much different from graphing calculators most math profs aren t going to let you take a graphing calculator in with you to the exam the problem isn t with the technology it s with our inability to control its use writing projects that take the place of exams in composition courses were traditionally done outside of class because there was no equivalent to the graphing calculator just simple calculators word processors now every student is relying on the ai graphing calculator to do their composition for them and that is hobbling their ability to understand and demonstrate mastery of the subject just like allowing math students to use graphing calculators on math exams would']

Name: 40_rule_nlp_based_ml
Representative_Docs: ['why can t you use both many ml systems run both rule based and ml based systems in parallel because in the real world no one technique or set of hyper parameters is going to get a perfect model that works for every condition your manager is obviously being silly because they want to say that they use ai ml but as already said you d be surprised at how many systems even especially at faang companies are mostly rule based what they do is run both in parallel choose what to use based on the confidence of the technique in a given scenario i e rule based system being good for product recommendations ml system being good at recommended based on previous purchases and then train their ml models to reduce gaps in coverage often using the rule based data to improve the model over time for your use case build the rule based system build a dataset based off of output from your rule based system and get them to play off of each other it s technically an ai system and there is scope to improve with improved sampled datasets over time', 'there are downsides to this approach one you have to maintain a complex rule based approach that could become irrelevant with changes in the data your rule based approach will be slow to react to changes in the data because you have to manually identify and make new rules for those changes this will be a vicious cycle of an ever growing complex rule based system that has to be managed by humans you can deploy an ml system that retrains a model automatically on new data and automatically track how well that updated model is performing this will automate a lot of future manual work i hate to say it but if you can t get an ml model to perform better than a rule based system then you either need more training data or you need a better model there s a reason why state of the art nlp systems use ml rather than rule based approaches your current system could be used for now to buy you some time to build an adequate ml system but it s not a great long term solution', 'i graduated with a mscs doing research in ml specifically nlp and it s been about months since i joined the startup that i m at the startup works with e commerce data and providing ai solutions to e commerce vendors one of the tasks that i was assigned was to design a system that receives a product name as input and outputs the product s category a very typical e commerce solution scenario my manager insisted that i use start of the art approaches in nlp to do this i tried this and that approach and got reasonable results but i also found that using a simple string matching approach using regular expressions and different logical branches for different scenarios not only achieves better performance but is much more robust it s been about a month since i ve been pitching this to my manager and he won t budge he was in disbelief that what i did was correct and keeps insisting that we double check i ve shown him charts where ml based approaches don t generalize edge cases where string matching outperforms ml which is very often showed that the cost of hosting a ml based approach would be much more expensive etc but nothing i don t know what else to do at this point there s pressure from above to deploy this project but i feel like my manager s indecisiveness is the biggest bottleneck i keep asking him what exactly it is that s holding him back but he just keeps saying well it s just such a simple approach that i m doubtful it ll be better than sota nlp approaches i m this close to telling him that in the real world ml is often not needed but i feel like that d offend him what else should i do in this situation i m feeling genuinely lost edit i m just adding this edit here because i see the same reply being posted over and over some form of but is string matching generalizable scalable and my conclusion for now is yes i m using a dictionary based approach with rules that i reviewed with some of my colleagues i have various datasets of product name category pairs from multiple vendors one thing that the language models have in common they all seem to generalize poorly across product names that follow different distributions why does this matter well we can never be sure that the data our clients input will follow the distribution of our training data on the other hand the rule based approach doesn t care what the distribution is as long as some piece of text matches the regex and the rule you re good to go in addition this model is handling the first part of a larger pipeline the results for this module are used for subsequent pieces that means that precision is extremely important which also means string matching will usually outperform neural networks that show high false positive rates']

Name: 41_papers_search_scholar_literature
Representative_Docs: ['researchrabbit ai is excellent doesn t try to generate anything new or rely on any stupid llms doesn t even try to rank results like google scholar just gives you incredibly useful network graphs of the papers that have cited and are cited in other papers you can search by papers authors or topic and it s all organized chronologically also this free', 'hi everyone here is the website i am building since this is primarily for academics science research community i would love to know if you think this is useful for you i have spent too much time on it at this point and i need a validation that this is not a fool s errand here are the key features available right now semantic search you can type in a long form description of research experiment or you can paste in a full abstract this is very different then classic search and usually yields better results similarity search you can click find similar on any paper in the search results and it will fish out all similar papers gpt claude summary of papers you can choose either llm to summarize any paper i am using gpt s dimensional vector embeddings that encode the meaning of all abstracts which results in incredibly good quality searches i had many people tell me they found papers they have not seen before if people find it useful i plan on onboarding other pre print arxivs bio med etc i am actually really curious if semantic cross search between difference arxivs might be insightful please let me know what you think also please let me know what features you want need and i will make sure to implement them i really appreciate everyone s everyone s opinion thanks', 'i did give one example researchrabbit try it out really you need to give it some fundamental papers of your topic that you found yourself and then it will give you a mind map of other papers and authors linked by both relevancy and if you wish chronological order for example i wrote about cultural intelligence and identified ang van dyne and rockstuhl as important authors of that field and linked a few studies of them then the program practically linked me the entire field of publications in a mapped out chronological order ranked by relevancy to the given foundational publications its insanely helpful i wrote my thesis in weeks and went through hundreds of sources that were relevant finally including no way would this have been possible by simply just reading everything not knowing exactly how relevant it d be and then figuring that out mind you this does not replace any of the research it just helps speed up the process that you ll then do yourself i still read all of the for example top most relevant papers but you see how simply having that list is immensely helpful afterwards i went and looked for some outliers that weren t given to me by the ai which is of course also important but there weren t many x b on the other hand really helpful ways to use for example chatgpt are if you re stuck on some phrases for example if i wrote one study of international students in australia reports metacognitive and behavioral cq to have a negative correlation with effectiveness and argues that students with particularly high levels of either dimension may become too confident on their cross cultural abilities resulting in a future lack of further adjustment negatively affecting cross cultural performance and think man this is wonky could i re phrase i can ask chatgpt for two or three iterations and pick the best one if you were fine with synonym websites like woxikon then this is really similar i urge you to ask yourself if you have a problem with this because you really think this is destroying academia or mostly just because its new by the way i did ask chatgpt to rephrase it just for the example and this was the result a study conducted on international students in australia found that there is a negative correlation between metacognitive and behavioral cultural intelligence cq and their effectiveness this study suggests that students with exceptionally high levels in either dimension may become overly self assured in their cross cultural abilities potentially leading to reduced motivation for further adjustment this in turn can have adverse consequences on their cross cultural performance in the future now it s up to us to find the best way to combine the two but it s a great source of inspiration x b what our professor tried to instil in us was the idea that we shouldn t try to treat ai as doing the work for us but that we should treat it as a personal assistant']

Name: 44_google docs_docs_google_history
Representative_Docs: ['my students did an at home timed writing test as in class essays one student s paper was totally off the prompt and he used high level vocabulary words which is very unlike him i asked to meet with him via zoom and i had him open up the google doc that he submitted well the paper paragraphs pages double spaced was written in minutes according to the google docs version history so it was obvious that he used ai and then copied and pasted it he also could not define many of the vocabulary words in the essay i may have to have them include a link to the google docs so that i can look at the version history of those that i suspect of cheating', 'here s what i ve done and it has worked in of cases so far in my syllabus it says students must write each paper in a single google doc from start to finish if i suspect your paper of being ai generated you need to give my access to the paper s version history on the spot if the version history does not reflect a typical writing process or if you did not write their entire paper in google docs i reserve the right to fail the paper in five of the six cases from last semester the students immediately crumbled when i saw their version history and pointed out that huge chunks of their paper just appeared in google docs and they then edited out tell tale marks paragraph headings things that are irrelevant to the assignment etc the sixth student doubled down and insisted that he wrote the paper bit by bit in another document and copied it over and that he always titled each of his paragraphs before doing so etc but i was still able to give him a zero for not doing all his writing in a single google doc i m sure they ll eventually figure out a workaround but it s been completely successful for me so far and i ll continue doing it', 'for anyone in this situation who doesn t know this google docs keeps version history information go to file version history see version history as long as you write in google docs rather than writing in some other program and copy and pasting it should auto save a version every few minutes so you can show a professor or whoever that you wrote the essay out paragraph by paragraph rather than pasting it in from an ai generator incidentally always use google docs for group projects the version history also shows you who wrote what i ve seen google docs histories that showed students doing nothing on the whole project but adding their name at the top if you re asking the prof to give you more time easier grading whatever because a group member didn t help google docs is your best argument']

Name: 46_exams_creative assignments_assessments_assessment
Representative_Docs: ['i mean this is not a hard issue to deal with at least in my class i talk to my students on have them demonstrate skills in front of me and have them write on pen in paper in front of me for assessments my assessments are worth of student grades i really don t care if they use ai for of the work they do at home though i heavily discourage it and there is a fair amount of in class work as well all it takes is or quizzes that the kids bomb because they re leaning too heavily on ai i offer retakes on everything first semester as long as the student conferences with me about their original try and shows proof of having studied then they take a new quiz problem solved hardly any more work for me', 'i am curious to hear your opinions on the matter many universities have banned the use of ai to write exams using plagiarism software to detect their use for example the use of chatgpt to write an essay can be detected due to writing patterns or the ai s use of external sources without citations however there are many other methods to use various types of ai without direct plagiarism where students might be using ai to create arguments presentations etc without learning material my question is how to we create assessments to gauge student learning given the expectation that ai will be used what do those assessments look like the future of research will utilise ai think as reviewer how can we expect students not to do the same even further on that train do we have a responsibility to train students to best use ai for research', 'it s understandable that you re feeling frustrated and disheartened about the impact of ai generated content on your teaching methods the use of ai in education is indeed changing the landscape of assessment and grading here are some possible responses acknowledge the frustration start by acknowledging their frustration and disappointment you can say something like i understand your frustration and it s perfectly valid to feel this way especially after dedicating so much time to creative assignments emphasize the value of creative assignments highlight the importance of the creative assignments you ve developed and how they have benefited students in various ways for example you could mention the critical thinking problem solving and real world application skills that these assignments promote acknowledge the efficiency of multiple choice exams while expressing your understanding you can also acknowledge the efficiency of multiple choice exams in terms of grading this can be seen as a silver lining making the workload more manageable for educators discuss potential solutions if you believe in the value of your creative assignments you might consider discussing possible ways to adapt or improve them in the face of ai generated content this could involve making assignments more focused on higher order thinking skills that ai can t easily replicate adapt to the changing landscape mention that the education landscape is evolving and adapting to these changes is essential exploring new methods to ensure academic integrity and student learning might be necessary mentioning the importance of staying current and adaptable can be reassuring highlight the importance of exam alternatives while recognizing the efficiency of multiple choice exams stress that there s still value in offering a variety of assessment methods including creative assignments and projects to cater to diverse learning styles and to foster a more comprehensive understanding of the material encourage feedback suggest an open dialogue with students and colleagues about these changes this could lead to innovative ways to balance traditional exams with creative assignments ensuring that students get a well rounded education in summary while understanding and empathizing with the frustration it s important to stress the value of creative assignments while acknowledging the changing educational landscape due to ai finding a balanced approach that combines traditional assessments with creative projects might be the way forward']

Name: 47_homeschooling_homeschool_child_miles
Representative_Docs: ['i think it is going to cause an entire paradigm shift of education i m posting this here to get perspective and input from people who have homeschool experience public school all my life though very interested in homeschooling in the future for my toddlers public and even private schools are becoming less and less palatable shootings violence woke ideology dissolving meritocracy cost inefficiency etc how many tax dollars are wasted on inefficient school systems with redundant and vestigial administrators it shouldn t take hours to learn hours worth of education there should already be a move to create an infrastructure for homeschooling so that tax dollars aren t continually wasted to make a glorified day care some company should make in the box type kits and possibly even have different subscription services so if parents do want their child s educational emphasis to be gender studies and drag queen story hour they can absolutely have that a la carte or if you think everybody poops is too scatalogical and the statue of david is porn there can be some puritanical content for that i don t think ai is there quite yet but it will be very very soon i think the big knock on the shelter in place for years was because all extra social interaction was frowned upon and a system fully unprepared to handle that situation was thrust into it and told to just do the best you can sorry for the delete and repost had a title issue i couldn t edit for whatever reason', 'why on earth would i want to use ai to teach my kids homeschooling means i get to handcraft their education and yeah it can be time consuming sometimes but having the ability to build lessons around my kids boosting their weaknesses keeping up with their strengths is the reason why i homeschool in the first place i m not sure how long you ve been homeschooling but there already are boxed curriculum sets both religious and secular there is a ton of curricula out there by individual subject as well and it takes very little time to build a full year s worth of academia would ai help maybe but it won t replace the hands on approach a parent or co op teacher can give an aside what is with the sudden rush of ai is so great posts i m seeing in homeschooling groups is it because ai is a shiny new toy everyone is fiddling with', 'i think ai has great potential but will ultimately do more harm than good in the us public education system education should focus on expanding one s knowledge and mind and growing character not just increasing productivity regarding inefficiency as someone who was homeschooled and who homeschools my own children it would be incredibly difficult to teach children a lesson in the same timeframe that i teach my own child if my child easily grasps a concept then a lesson may only last minutes especially if i ve tailored it to her learning style from a classroom management standpoint it s just impossible to do the same especially if they go from room to room so it s a bit unfair to expect anywhere near the same level of efficiency as a home education they re always going to be wildly different learning and teaching experiences as for infrastructure i don t mind taxes going to a public education system although i d love to see the system completely overhauled so that money is used more effectively homeschooling will never be an option for every family it s impossible for some public education does have essential value in society what i don t want is government money tied up in my homeschooling at all what the government pays for the government controls homeschooling is a way to keep the government out of my child s education i m convinced that what the government touches the government ruins at this point i do think more private scholarships to help homeschool families in need would be a good thing some already exist but at the rate homeschooling is growing in popularity need already exceeds availability there s so much curriculum out there religious neutral or secular you can easily teach your child whatever you wish at home in any learning teaching style box kits for all traditional subjects at grade level i m sure there s a drag story hour on youtube or a library that streams it if you don t live nearby parents are always going to have to search for the things they want to teach that s part of the job i mean the david statue is porn vs gender emphasis kind of material exists just not from the same company but i wouldn t trust a company that sold woke gender material and bible based curriculum because they re such opposing viewpoints and ideologies i fully expect them to get the bible stuff wrong honestly i think you re looking at homeschooling as a system to replace the public system but that s not what home education should be it s a lifestyle choice that looks and operates differently for every family it s not an institution nor can it ever be']

Name: 49_social good_social_field_society
Representative_Docs: ['the most social good you can do with ai is to productionize in a better way and make it more robust in a technical sense most of the rest is putting it frankly feel good fluff i ve said as much face to face with timnit gebru so i feel like i have a little bit of street cred on the matter personally i don t subscribe to this weird notion that ai will somehow magically make society worse at least not more than the steam engine or the internet it will change society in unpredictable ways grow the economy a bit and we ll deal with whatever issues arise the adjustment will be painful for some people but we don t stop adopting cars just because it displaces farriers there is a lot of work to be done on minimizing specific social harm but you need something much much more concrete than ai for social good the people i ve seen in this field often have incredibly limited understanding of economics and society and their work is consequently often mostly performative in nature if you really want to work on ai policy get a graduate degree in economics or political science if you want to create social good i think there is plenty of that in cv especially in the context of self driving and reducing the social costs of commuting', 'i recently attended cvpr in vancouver and as someone who is deeply passionate about computer vision it was an exhilarating experience however something happened during my time here that left me with a mix of emotions and thoughts that i wanted to share with all of you x b a few days ago while exploring the city i stumbled upon an avenue where i encountered a large number of homeless people engaged in what appeared to be the consumption of crack or meth it was shocking to witness this many individuals in one place openly using hard drugs during daylight as if it were an everyday occurrence this stark contrast between the reality outside the convention center and the world of cutting edge technology inside got me thinking about the impact i m truly making in society x b you see my field of expertise lies in autonomous vehicles a domain that some may consider a bourgeois field detached from the immediate social issues around us while my passion for knowledge and pushing the boundaries of technology has driven me towards academia this encounter has left me pondering whether i am truly contributing to something bigger and better for humanity x b recently i stumbled across a fascinating area known as ai for social good it seems to be a field where ai and related technologies are being applied to tackle societal challenges and make a positive impact on the world the idea of switching my focus after completing my phd to contribute to this field has started to occupy my thoughts x b i m reaching out to you all to hear your perspectives on this matter how do you deal with the strange feeling of disconnection from society that can arise from being deeply involved in a specialized field like computer vision have any of you considered transitioning to a domain like ai for social good or similar areas if so what factors influenced your decision x b i believe that computer vision and ai have the potential to transform society in profound ways however the encounter with the homeless population in vancouver has made me question the direct impact my work is having on the broader issues that our world faces i m eager to hear your thoughts and experiences on this topic', 'economics for social good healthcare for social good education for social good agriculture for social good data science for social good all of those sounds rediculous']

Name: 51_grade_rubrics_cheating_rubric
Representative_Docs: ['i agree with this as part of the definition of plagiarism but the notion of academic integrity is somewhat wider than just plagiarism a lot depends on the purpose of the assignment using a spelling checker on a spelling bee is cheating using it on a paper for a philosophy class is not using a grammar checker in an esl class that is teaching grammar is cheating using it on biology lab report is not similarly using an ai tool to clean up writing would be cheating in a writing course but not necessarily for a senior thesis if the tool being used interferes with what the student is supposed to be learning then using it is cheating if what the tool does produces a better product but does not interfere with the intended learning then using it is not cheating i think that a lot of the differences of opinion about the use of ai writing tools come from different unstated assumptions about what the learning outcomes are supposed to be that is one good reason to be more explicit about what your course s learning outcomes really are', 'i don t interpret them because i don t use them my school doesn t even have turnitin so i have always been completely on my own for plagiarism detection and now for ai detection too almost like my school doesn t want us pesky professors detecting cheating and upsetting c u s t o m e r s students by confronting them with evidence of their cheating i teach rhet comp so yeah students are definitely using ai one thing i ve done is make very detailed and particular rubrics with specific points allotments attached to about dozen criteria under the various categories of content rhetorical strategies research quality and integration and style i grade the ai papers the same as i do the others and using the rubric criteria those papers never earn above a d most fail and i mean sure i m sure there s some slipping thru that i m not detecting as ai but honestly once i started using these super detailed rubrics the overall grade average of written assignments in my courses has plummeted which i take as a good thing since a decade of grade inflation had taken hold when i was using more holistic rubrics what sucks though is that my department hates that i am using these rubrics or even rubrics at all so it s only a matter of time before i m told outright not to use them or forced to follow a standard syllabus oh well at least i ve fought the good fight while i can', 'it can catch copy paste but once students figure that out they ll just use ai on their phone and manually type the ai generated words into their doc from your or draftback s perspective they re writing live trying to defeat ai this way is a losing battle you can t detect ai writing you can detect bad ai writing but that s about it and the whole idea of homework is dead i teach business mgmt at a community college and have international students cheating has been rampant forever now i m using dr ethan mollick s ai policy wharton mba program check out his blog oneusefulthing org students are free to use ai in fact encouraged to use ai to generate their answers to various assignments they need to show and explain the prompts they used and the written submission is only worth a fraction of the whole assignment grade the bulk of the grade comes from their presentation of their answer and the bulk of that grade comes from impromptu critical thinking questions about their answer if company x were to follow your recommendation and things went wrong what would the worst case scenario look like in what circumstances could that happen as just one example students are told in advance that this is how they ll be graded and to expect difficult questions they don t know the questions in advance if the student simply uses ai to generate an answer but they don t deeply understand the answer such that they can reply competently to unexpected critical thinking questions then they ll fail the assignment as a result performance in my classes has gone through the roof students are forced to think critically about their subject matter in a way that most of them never did before my most mediocre dopey students are now performing at a high b grade level and my top students are smashing it out of the ballpark i had a colleague say to me yeah but can t they just get chatgpt to give them a bunch of critical thinking questions and answers and so they ll just use that to prepare themselves to answer your questions my response yeah good for them if they ve learned they ve learned why do i care whether it was from the textbook my lecture or chatgpt the outcome is the outcome and if they can genuinely demonstrate it they pass']

Name: 52_grading_feedback_grade_perusall
Representative_Docs: ['this is a classic teacher problem i feel your pain it s that moment when you find a solution that s almost perfect but requires so much extra work it defeats the purpose the easier for the computer to recognize a perfect little circle than to parse a kid s handwritten answer on a regular test page you re looking for something that uses optical character recognition ocr on an entire document and that s a much more complex and expensive technology that said while you re looking for a solution for multiple choice tests i can tell you about what has saved me on the even bigger problem grading written assignments as an ai for english teachers the essays are what keep me up at night i ve found that finding an ai grading tool that helps with the feedback part is even
