Name: 59_writing_paper_research_writing papers
Representative_Docs: ['hey there using ai in research is definitely a hot topic and it s understandable that conferences are emphasizing transparency ai tools can be great research assistants boosting efficiency but they shouldn t replace the researcher s critical thinking and writing personally i find them useful for tasks like summarizing lengthy papers identifying key themes and even brainstorming outlines however i always thoroughly review and rewrite ai generated content to ensure accuracy and originality my work is always my own there are many tools out there each with its own strengths and weaknesses if you re looking for something beyond basic summarization you might want to check out platforms like paper pilot xyz it offers features like research organization ai powered insights and even latex integration potentially streamlining your whole writing process remember though any ai output needs careful review and editing appropriate citation and disclosure following your conference s guidelines always carefully consider a tool s capabilities and limitations before using it', 'hey there fellow scholars of r askacademia if you re anything like me you know the struggle of diving deep into academic papers only to realize you ve spent days and haven t even started the actual writing part sound familiar so i ve been quietly tinkering away on something to help us all out drumroll please introducing your ai powered writing co pilot why you ll love it curiosity driven exploration not only can you ask it questions but it can also generate endless questions and threads for you to explore dive deep without the endless scrolling through databases depth not just breadth this isn t about quick summaries this assistant generates full length articles essays or reports you could practically write a thesis with it you take the wheel think of it as writing with a co pilot who s read every academic journal you get to guide the ai to generate precisely what you need but wait there s more imagine directing that saved time and energy into groundbreaking research or maybe even catching up on some sleep a rare academic luxury more than a tool this is your research writing partner the ai doesn t just churn out content haphazardly it s meticulously researched and crafted and you re in the driver s seat co authoring your next great piece with an incredibly smart tool so who s up for revolutionizing their academic writing process your early feedback would mean the world to me check it out at the pinned post here stay tuned for the official launch trust me you won t want to miss it stay curious sal', 'i m assuming you re being hyperbolic by giving the and numbers or whomever you re used to being around is just not very good at scholarship a few points in the us the nih has banned the use of ai to write grant reviews i m guessing other funding agencies either have or will do so in the near future if you need ai to write emails what have you been doing all this time i ve heard this before but seriously if you can be bothered to work on your writing to make it personable and coherent enough so you don t embarrass yourself with your colleagues how can you be expected to put enough thought into writing a grant proposal or a thoughtful properly referenced review looking up synonyms is my favorite example people that interact with other humans and understand the finer points of their fields often fail when looking at thesaurus com picking words that technically are the same but are obviously not the correct choice for the circumstance from what i ve seen with ai it s definitely not an improvement i ve said this other places and i ll say it here if you don t know your own work and how it fits into the current body of literature well enough to accurately convey that to your peers without the help of ai how can i trust you to use the proper controls and do proper reporting using ai as a tool to improve yourself is different from using it to save time or be more productive why the hell spend all the effort of asking questions to a machine if you have to do that in addition to proofreading that and correcting it it s like the fact about taking notes that a lot of students and even professors want to ignore writing to take your notes from a class or a seminar has been shown over and over to be far more effective for long term learning than mindlessly typing a copy of every word a speaker is presenting ai has a place and will continue to but if you re going to use the example of old people saying the internet was a fad let s take a look at wikipedia when you want to know if christopher walken was on the boat when natalie wood died get a quick synopsis of the war of the roses a summary of pet semetary or what the mineral composition of an emerald is wikipedia is your go to if you want real answers for the differences between apoptosis necropoptosis pyroptosis and necrosis their negative regulators and what positive controls to use wikipedia shouldn t be where you spend your time']

Name: 64_professor_gptzero_grade_paper
Representative_Docs: ['the dean ended up saying to contact the head of the department so i did and after hours of not getting a response from both i contacted my advisor and he gave me some useful assignment name because he suspects i used ai to complete the assignment he specifically said this decision was made with help from a program called gptzero a program that claims to identify whether a piece of writing is ai generated or not i am hurt at this accusation and frustrated because my submission for assignment name was my own original work and i did not use dishonest methods to write it i am especially frustrated because professor x is treating gptzero as a reliable source to determine if a piece of writing was written by ai or not it is established that gptzero is vulnerable to producing false positives and labelling known human writing as ai generated text here are some sources that discuss how gptzero is unreliable list of sources here is a social media post from a forum of college professors discussing how gptzero is not a reliable source to determine if student submitted work was written by ai link to r professors post i am upset at this hasty accusation based on an unreliable source and i am requesting that the accusation be retracted and my grade on the assignment be reinstated please email me if you need any information from me i am more than happy to provide anything to defend my innocence thanks u camisrutt', 'professor here who has caught several students using ai to write essays definitely go to the chair of the department or dean with this and make your case it s unlikely the professor is out to get you or anything but there are several things i feel like i need to say to this sub i don t know any professor who uses ai detectors as a default you know how much work that would be like we don t have enough to do already especially if that professor has a hundred essays at the end of the semester and limited time to submit final grades to the university it s just not feasible to run a check on everything rather here s what most likely happened professor reads essay and something looks suspicious professors read hundreds of essays every semester they get a sense for how students write given the rise of students using ai to write essays professor runs it through a checker and lo and behold it confirms a suspicion now the professor has a choice i m not condoning any professor who gives the student a zero and closes the book there should always be transparency and opportunities to contest but it seems most people here are pretty quick to condemn the professor about what really might be university policy personally i actually break this policy by not immediately reporting students i catch but i don t think it s fair to condemn professors who are abiding by university policy do you all understand how many students actually do use ai you think professors should just let this go it s our job to try to grade fairly and it s not fair that some students just decide not to do the work for all you saying that ai detectors are bullshit no they re not are they reliable of course not but they are decent enough tools when i ve had suspicions from reading an essay if i run it through different ai checks and every one of them says the essay is to ai written that s not bullshit it s not proof but it s not bullshit i give my students a zero explain to them my concerns tell them about the ai check and then give them the choice to contest it or take the zero but if they contest it it becomes official which can go on their record no student so far has attempted to contest it it s hard for me to come to any conclusion other than they know they were caught and they know under scrutiny it would be revealed so they take the zero because they can still scrape by with a passing grade all of you complaining about professors relying on ai detectors don t seem to understand how much work we do how many essays we read and how stressful it is to try to grade equitably we re not out to get you we re trying to increase accountability']

Name: 69_pong_chess_ball_moves
Representative_Docs: ['one thing you haven t done is define your problem space that s kind of important ok so you want to build an ai to play pong is this a game of pong that you ve coded yourself and the ai has full access to the variables involved in the ball movement is this an ai that runs on the same machine and can move the paddle by providing inputs do you want to reverse engineer the game and get the ball position from memory or do you need to use computer vision techniques to read to ball position and respond accordingly do you need to evade any anti cheat protections that may exist or is this an ai that needs to run entirely separately from the pong game itself do you need to build a hardware solution with a camera for input and robotics to physically move the controller is this a pong game where the paddle can move freely and need only be positioned to match the ball s vertical position or does the paddle have a limited movement speed and the ai needs to predict the ball s trajectory factoring in wall bounces are you wanting to manually guide the ai based on the rules of pong or do you want to develop one of those deep reinforcement learning ais where you really only give it a vague goal and the button mapping and let the ai experiment of billions of iterations until it figures out how to achieve that goal learning how to define the whole problem break it down into manageable logical tasks then solve each individual problem is kind of a big deal in programming when learned the typical way but if you re going out of your way to be different good luck', 'welcome to the world of chess engines my favorite sub field i got started with chess engines because like you i was interested in machine learning ai however you ll quickly find out that most chess engines do not use machine learning some do though but only for a specific part here s a brief overview of components to a chess engine move generation you need a fast way to take the state of a chess board and generate all legal moves given a specific side white or black game tree searching once you can find all legal moves at a given position you can move on to the meat and potatoes of chess engines game tree searching imagine a tree data structure where each node is a position of a chess board a parent node a links to a child node b if there exists a legal move that can take you from a to b the heart of chess engine is searching this tree to find the best possible move board evaluation in order to deem a move as the best move you need to be able to determine whether a move is good or bad to do this you need to be able to evaluate a position this means that given the state of the board your algorithm should determine whether white or black has an advantage and by how much the way a chess engine works is that it will be given a position and start searching a game tree given that position in theory you search every single position that is reachable from the starting position and then every position reachable from all of those positions and so on so forth you evaluate each position and you find the position that has the highest board evaluation for whichever side is to play and you take the first move in the tree that leads to that position this is a chess engine in its most basic form most of your optimization will come from narrowing the game tree by filtering out positions that do not need to be searched there are a ton of techniques to do this i won t go into them now re but i feel like watching a tutorial is cheating watching a tutorial is not cheating in fact think of it simply as reference someone else has already built a chess engine before you should look at it and figure out why they did it a certain way once you look at enough you ll learn what little things set different programs apart there s never anything wrong with watching a tutorial to learn even if you just copy their code the first time you can always write it on your own again changing whatever you want also here s some useful resources this is the chess programming wiki it will be your best friend in chess engine development this is a subreddit dedicated to chess programming i am active on here sometimes as well would definitely recommend putting future questions in here this is a chess gui that easily lets you test your custom engines your engine should implement a protocol called uci in order to talk with almost any chess gui', 'well pong is too simple and i completely agree with you but i m working with limited knowledge so thank you for baring with me x b both pongs will have limited movement speed along with limited space ofcourse so if the pong perfectly follows the ball and doesn t take into account the wall bounces or speed of the ball it might not be able to reach the ball even with alot of reaction time so the pong might need to not only know where to hit the ball like left center right but also where to go after it hits the ball which actually might be able to be programmed by alot of brute force and no ai but i m not sure which is best x b how the chess ai is able to play is by analyzing millions of games and potential positions and doing multiple consecutive moves to see which is best alongside alot of rules set on it like obviously bad moves and different tactics x b as for pong a good strategy for example is like aiming for corners more more wall bounces calculating which bounce is best to confuse the opponent etc or even feints like going for a side but making the ball bounce on the wall to the furthermost right']

and beyond fee us link', 'tl dr after years as a data scientist data analyst i discovered i like to work on the full solution of a business problem not only the data part however i m still quite novice in swe and a jack of all trades type so i m having a hard time finding the right career path leaning towards full stack python react webdev but keeping an open mind background i studied in europe as business engineer mainly business oriented with a good base of sciences maths stats physics i then worked for years as a generalist data scientist i say generalist because it varied a lot dashboarding prediction models forecasting optimisation segmentations lot s of topics never went super deep in any most coding experience was self taught googling and trial and error for a year now i m working as a project engineer i work on computer vision projects but do a mix of managing swe project management gathering requirements and a some coding why a career change all these years i ve often hesitated to quit and start a full stack engineering learning why because in my job as a data scientist i had a couple of projects where a gui was needed so i quickly developed very basic solutions in flask web apps i also tinkered around with tkinter for desktop apps i really enjoyed bringing the exact customisation and flexibility the client was looking for helping them have a more productive day with a tool i helped create i also enjoyed debugging why something wasn t working as expected and fixing the issue i can spend hours not seeing the time pass when working on a solution that i know will bring someone value in my current job i m doing a lot of lobbying cost estimations powerpoint presentations quite a bit of managing and it really drains my energy i enjoy discussing projects with non technical users but currently too much time goes into project admin company politics and not enough time focussing on the product itself i enjoy finding innovative and creative solutions to a problem but the problem has to be clearly scoped why i m hesitant to make the switch i really feel like a jack of all trades master of none and i wonder if i ll be able to keep up with people for whom computer science has always been a passion some examples to illustrate why i m hesitant and then some counter points i tell myself on these i like creating a frontend but i m not a perfectionist i m afraid i m not enough detail oriented to choose the exact best button placement font colors responsive design etc however i really like to put myself in the position of the end user and think what would be practical and when i see the benefit of putting a layout a certain way i suddenly do become obsessed with getting it just right and can spend hours getting it just right this is why i lean more towards business applications and not marketing oriented projects i m myself more interested in making a practical product than making it look nice i ve implemented a really small backend and optimising the queries is not my first concern i tend to wait until the database is overloaded slow before optimizing i think a lot of this is also due to the fact i knew they would only get low traffic i actually enjoy the optimization process when i know it makes a big impact on user experience the bigger data pipelines i ve coded in python i feel often end up quite spaghetti code ish but i m really looking to improve i started reading books like clean code from r martin i see the value of clean code for readability maintainability etc refactoring is not something i dread i actually enjoy it but it s really hard to get right i never had any code reviews so i think it would help a lot to have expert opinions a lot of more technical concepts i read up on and forget soon after it doesn t really click these are searches such as what is a kernel what is a daemon what s the difference between ngnix and gunicorn difference between multi threading and multi processing etc i more concerned about making it work than how it works but again when it starts to matter for user experience i enjoy diving really deep exploring leet code challenges i often get stuck in finding the most efficient implementation i really enjoy doing a lot of things computer vision optimisations frontend backend geo spatial data cloud architectures etc it s hard to choose in what i should specialize in summary i m driven by the solution i m providing i love creating a tailor made solutions and keep it flexible scalable etc i feel like coding gives me this freedom to bring any idea into reality the downside is i m not inherently driven by how something works i only figure it out to make it work advice i m looking for for all the reasons above i consider going for full stack developer to fit this jack of all trades profile i d focus on python for backend i already have a good base and i really like the flexibility of the language i d add javascript with react for the frontend learn the basics of nodejs play around with docker and cloud platforms to deploy it all i read a bit about the net environments maybe it s more suited for building business apps are there people with similar experiences alternatives i m considering integration developer focus more on integrating existing products and api s i really like building on top of other developments to extract maximum value i don t need to develop every piece myself i m good at building on top of others machine learning engineer but focus on proven technologies and going to production right now i often work on too experimental projects that stay at proof of concept level application owner i have a good background mix in tech and business but i really think it s too much company politics for me and i d miss building technical lead probably not senior enough and would be too much people management solutions architect lack of experience i think i d prefer building myself any other suggestions for company size i m leaning towards smaller mid size between and employees i like the startup mentality in even smaller ones but i think i ll learn more from other colleagues in a slightly bigger company i feel bigger companies are going to ask for too specialized roles rather than the full stack role i m looking for agree or is your experience different maybe i should just take the first software developer offer that i can get and pivot later i actually got an offer for a junior python javascript developer position a year ago but i went for my current employer because i really liked their computer vision projects but the reality is that here i m not coding much anymore and i really miss it for my salary here i don t think they ll let me in on their full developer roles with my limited experience thank you in advance for any suggestions and if no reactions at least you will have helped me to write my thoughts down']

Name: 71_code_programming_better_possible
Representative_Docs: ['for some time at least i know there will obviously still be an expectation that people need to know how to code well that will likely always be the case if you can t actually code you would have no way to debug issues ai is still going to write code full of bugs or unintended behaviour the code generated by ai can only be as perfect as the requirements and the requirements themselves will have flaws the whole reason we invented programming languages was because natural human language was too imprecise for machines and i don t think that will ever stop being true ai is just another tool in our toolbox and you will have to be able to make due when that tool inevitably fails you i think people are way overselling what ai will be able to do for us', 'no matter how many times it becomes incrementally better artificial narrow intelligence will never be enough to steal the rug from normal creative works such as software engineering agi isn t just a marketing term it s the complete end goal for ai which is still nowhere close to where we are an artificial intelligence capable of understanding and learning any task that human beings can ani on the other hand can be impressive but if you think that it can get anywhere near being able to comprehend the entirety of our works i don t think you re thinking about ani at all it doesn t take just getting incrementally better to get from one to the other ani will always be completely flawed in the fact that it can only churn inputs and outputs without any sense of a higher form of understanding the point is that it isn t just about churning outputs based off of certain inputs that oversimplification overlooks the entire process sure you can reduce everything to just inputs and outputs if you tried but many of those require actual functioning intellect to string together think about teaching a monkey a language it can incrementally get better and speak well but will it ever have a high enough level of intellect that would enable it to write a novel there s a big difference ai can eventually get to that point but a better ani is no indication that we got even a step closer', 'first the term ai is used for things that aren t considered ai so we attribute that it s intelligent we talk about ai for game engines and ai for things playing chess but it s ai in the loosest sense also we have lots of tools like doing method completion and flagging syntax errors and so forth that would be considered almost like ai but are merely tools for example how does something like chatgpt really understand something if your boss gives you a program to write and does a bad job can chatgpt write that code in any case for an ide just because you forgot the name of a method and the ide suggests it for you does that mean you re not programming these days cars can do lane detection and collision detection at this point are you driving yes you still are yes maybe you ll eventually not have to drive and it will be like taking a taxi but programming is harder than that it may mean more people can program but do you know what happened when we got better tools to program we wrote harder programs we pushed what was possible the kinds of programs you wrote in are far simpler than the ones you can write now because technology has made it possible to write complex programs if we have better ai tools then we write more complex programs it s even possible that the programming languages we use now are inadequate to write the programs of tomorrow who knows can an ai come up with a new programming language and what would that look like']

Name: 74_ml_cogsci_degree_cs
Representative_Docs: ['i also want to break into ai ml data science field many many jobs seek a phd for research roles which makes sense since it is a research degree for other ml engineering and data science roles i almost always see ms preferred which has prompted me to pursue an ms i also work as a data analyst currently and in interviews what i struggle overcoming is not having prediction forecasting model work as part of my experience education and personal projects has not been sufficient so far for me they don t even want to talk about it i think statistics is what you want to study but it might depend on what you plan to make your brand you usually learn programming while studying stats a cs degree will have you developing applications and you might find that you are more attractive for those types of roles rather than researching type roles that want to do inference if you go down that path you will definitely need to become a technical person lol you would be the technical expert as an ai researcher that s all the insight i have i m still trying to learn myself it s so much to learn coming from a nontechnical background as well', 'i m about a year into a computer science degree applied linguistics minor also interested in ai and ml i am interested in cognitive science but if you are specifically angling for ai from what i can tell a background in computer science and math will get you further and enable you to explore cogsci later cognitive science is a combination of a lot of different really interesting disciplines and i think it would be more useful to choose a few of those and move from those in the direction of cogsci than to study cogsci and move toward one of its disciplines i would have considered a cogsci major if my school offered it but i think a compsci background with a cogsci or related minor will be a more solid foundation if i were you i d go computer science major data science minor but i m also only a student so talk to your professors and advisors too', 'it doesn t really matter here is what you need to do if you re set on grad school take as many hard math courses as you can and try to get a s in all of them columbia is known for grade inflation so you should shoot for a in at least your major not a huge problem if you miss slightly but don t take easy courses just to pump up your gpa top programs have become hyper competitive so anything less is slightly frowned up focus on linear algebra discrete math and mathematical statistics if you feel like you can handle more tensor algebra pdes stat mechanics or functional analysis are all good to have s a lot of innovation in ml is very math heavy so you should aim to take math courses just to demonstrate that you can handle them whichever courses you take start searching for an undergraduate advisor immediately for undergrads i d shoot for older faculty members slightly past their prime so to speak they ll have more time for you personally and they ll do great on the how do you rank this candidate among your other students part of the letter try to get some work done with at least one untenured guy though once you find someone talk to their grad students try to put together a research idea of your own do whatever you can to get a paper out of it keep the aforementioned advisor informed about your progress grad admissions people aren t stupid they will look at what classes you took nobody cares if you take them as a cs major a physics major or a journalism major fwiw the only real connection in ai and physics these days that i can think of is quantum computing computational physics is a relatively mature field and doesn t involve a lot of black box works with some exceptions ofc the problem with quantum computing is that your grad school experience has virtually zero translation into the broader private sector so it s a risky field to get in']

Name: 75_teachers_laws_school_internet
Representative_Docs: ['i had a bunch of students come in today th grade and they all said i hate school why do we even come here after their classes i told them let s talk about it i listened to everyone who had their hands up and the general complaints were why do we have to stay in school for hours a day why do the teachers suck at teaching then a story about another teacher who barely understood what she was saying why do child labor laws exist when i can learn more from working why don t teachers care about our stress mental health why do i need to learn any subject here when i won t use it in real life then there were a lot of comments about ai the internet teaching more than teachers i told them some answers about the parents having to work for that long that teachers sometimes have to cover subjects they didn t major in and about how it s difficult to give attention to just one student in a room of etc so i was wondering what would your answers be for these kids i tried to tell them that the internet and ai won t be forever and aren t always accurate they said the internet had taught them more than any teacher that s saddening to hear from a kid s mouth i remember loving school and i was excited to learn something new even when i told them they needed to learn the basics for the future none of them seemed to care they said they could just learn through doing it and not sitting in a classroom if they don t know why they come then it is no surprise that they re doing poorly how do i motivate them', 'why do child labor laws exist when i can learn more from working child labor laws made it so one parent working had to be paid enough for the whole family to survive businesses would have no workers otherwise because children no longer had to work they could get an education and move up the class ladder without this if you were born into a poor family with an undesirable job too bad that was your future x b why do we have to stay in school for hours a day x b on the other hand when both parents started working businesses realized with double the work force they only had to pay each parent half as much now that both parents have to work they vote for representatives that will increase the amount of time the government will watch their children while they are at work why do the teachers suck at teaching then a story about another teacher who barely understood what she was saying currently we assign value based on testing and the tests for teachers test for content they do not test for teaching because of this educational institutions train teachers to pass tests based on content as opposed to refining their teaching skills if we just trusted teachers with passion to teach and gave them support it would be quite a different system i am not saying many teachers are bad at teaching but we all could benefit from a more teaching centric system why don t teachers care about our stress mental health x b where i teach the teachers do care about students mental health often more than the parents often more than the community often more than the students themselves why do i need to learn any subject here when i won t use it in real life x b i use every subject every day i used english when i formatted easy to follow directions to both my students and parents i am using what i learned in english as i write this i used math when i made sure i had enough money for my groceries i used algebra to figure out how much everyone owed for a meal i used social studies when i thought about the cause and effect of previous national school policies and how to improve them or how to use my vote i used math and science when i made my pourover coffee this morning with grams of grounds and ml of water and a required temperature to brew i use art and science when i process the speculative fiction i like to read if you want to only use one skill for the rest of your life like digging ditches that may be an option but education and therefore me your teacher exist to give you more options then there were a lot of comments about ai the internet teaching more than teachers x b i am not sure what to make of this ai doesn t teach it just retains knowledge that has already been created and the internet the internet is a tool that you can learn to use if you don t know how to use it i feel like it only teaches how to divide and hate can you learn to use the internet to learn sure but that involves knowing how to learn of which most people learn how to learn from', 'why do we have to stay in school for hours a day hours labor hours recreation hours rest was the balance suggested by robert owen at the turn of the th century it would be the driving goal of the labor movement in europe and america for the rest of the th century this was in sharp contrast to hour shifts one could expect in some early factories and mines why do the teachers suck at teaching then a story about another teacher who barely understood what she was saying i don t know the teacher so i don t know their story they may have too many classes to prep and might be so exhausted they didn t keep their lessons straight they may be a brand new teacher or new to the subject and trying to keep up with the curriculum right ahead of their students they may be a long term substitute or someone without a degree or teaching credential at this point with all the shortages for teachers there are a myriad of possibilities but if pressed to answer the students without context i would likely say that people make mistakes sometimes and if it is not a daily occurrence it most likely was simply that there are also students who i hear say that some of their teachers don t teach them but what that means is that they are forced to work individually to investigate and analyze their work on their own rather than the answers being gift wrapped to them i d likely ask more details to find the right answer for them while being professional why do child labor laws exist when i can learn more from working encourage them to read oliver twist if they can child labor laws exist to make sure that children are damaged by harsh working conditions pigeon holed into low skill work that they will never be able to sustain themselves or a family on break cycles of poverty and class rigidity and to protect jobs and wages for adults why don t teachers care about our stress mental health we live in a perpetual cycle of earlier generations having perceived higher academic and social expectations than the subsequent ones which leads those in the earlier generations to not understand why when they ve watered down and given the newer generation a trip on easy street they can t even manage it it s what our grandparents said of our parents our parents said of us what we say of them i do care about the mental health of my students but i also know that many of them lack the self efficacy time management organization and personal pride they need in their school work responsibilities to do things properly i give recommendations to them regularly on how to amend these practices to have less stress but they rarely choose to prioritize them because they as you noted op do not see the value in the work so they do not value planning ahead for it why do i need to learn any subject here when i won t use it in real life i assume you live in the united states so i will address it based upon that context the current structure of education in the us is built on the idea of preparing students to be well rounded citizens who have a basic background in how universal and global systems work science how logic and critical thinking works math understanding of our culture and shared stories english the past of our country and traditions to better take an active political role in our republic history and how to live a healthy life pe in secondary education there are more opportunities for students to have the chance to be introduced to more niche or unique types of study which may spark interest in a field or an occupation electives unlike either europe or east asia where high stakes testing and tracking at a young age prepare students for a profession the united states prefers to make its citizens a jack of all trades a master of none by the time they are free to choose what their outcome or profession in adulthood might be this gives them far more flexibility in their future for what work they might choose in life especially if the first or second jobs they try do not work and they need to be trained again in something else then there were a lot of comments about ai the internet teaching more than teachers to be able to pull knowledge from the internet is one thing but to be able to utilize knowledge critical thought and creativity to create something is very different my students even my ap ones struggle mightily with critical thinking if it isn t something we have said concretely they struggle rephrase something ask them to apply the knowledge and extrapolate or try to understand why something happened and they crumble our students when they use the internet are good semi competent at fetching data not at training their brains to make connections develop a knowledge base or to use it for greater purposes they often aren t even good at searching for information on the internet either and only know how to read what is served up to them by the google search results page rather than reading the articles or webpages that are actually linked there']

Name: 76_restaurant_priority_air_predictive
Representative_Docs: ['a couple clarifying questions is this data from an actual building is your hvac discharge temp just from the central unit or at each individual room most systems you see are Name: 84_writing_student writing_tas_students
Representative_Docs: ['best not to rely on a single tool then and to keep it straight if the tool says something is ai content or better if multiple separate tools say so then that s a very strong indicator that it s ai enough that i d feel comfortable acting on it provided it also sets off my own radar but it doesn t work the other way a low score doesn t mean they didn t use ai ultimately the best way is to talk to the student and see if they understand and to look for changes over time a student whose writing ability suddenly changes or varies wildly from assignment to assignment etc', 'thanks for the input your experience with ai detection and your thoughts seem to correlate with mine and this problem is especially prominent in our field asking for a solution i still have none and it has been quite some time since i posted this and started looking for a solution before i could find one the students found the solution they are writing disgustingly poor quality reports and assignments on purpose in order to avoid false positives many students of mine have been complaining about having to write poor quality texts and intentionally leaving typos or punctuation errors they are supposed to be practicing precise scientific writing before they graduate but they are doing the exact opposite it is mostly tas grading the lab reports so i do not see this as often but i can definitely observe this shift towards poor writing when i m grading assignments and take home exams i always encourage my students to write properly and precisely without any mistakes and i tell them not to worry about false positives i am very sensitive about this issue now although we do not tell the students about it i spoke with the tas and we actually disabled the turnitin s ai detection feature for my class tas are sending me the student papers they are suspicious of and i check them individually but that s not the case with my colleagues they just deduce points or give a fat so students keep writing poorly this ai detection did more bad than good in my opinion students are not improving how will they write papers when they come to the point where they need to publish their work i am concerned but i cannot do anything other than trying to correct this problem for my class which does not help much', 'as a teacher it can be challenging to identify when a student submits an assignment written by an artificial intelligence program here are some potential indications unnatural language patterns ai written papers may contain peculiar phrasing or usage that humans don t typically use while advanced models like gpt have made strides in generating human like text slight discrepancies can still emerge inconsistent tone or writing style if the student s writing style abruptly changes within the paper or if it doesn t match their known writing style it might be ai generated note that this method is not foolproof as students may also change their writing styles due to other factors such as improvement over time different sources of inspiration or if they had help from a peer or a tutor lack of personal insight or reflection ai doesn t have personal experiences or emotions so if a paper lacks individual insights unique perspectives or personal examples it may be a clue that it was ai generated inaccurate or irrelevant information ai especially text generating models like gpt may not fully grasp the context of the question and can sometimes generate information that is inaccurate misleading or not relevant if the information provided does not directly answer the assignment s question or if it seems off topic it might be due to ai overly complex sentences and excessive use of jargon sometimes ai can overcomplicate sentences or use more technical or sophisticated language than necessary if a paper uses an excessive amount of complex language or technical jargon especially if it s not used correctly it might be a sign of ai generation missing citations since ai generates information based on a vast dataset it doesn t provide specific sources or references for the data a lack of in text citations especially when they re required could be a clue to ai involvement as a teacher fostering a strong rapport with your students can also help you get to know their unique voices and styles which could make it easier to spot discrepancies if they attempt to use ai for their assignments']

Name: 88_phd_industry_academia_research
Representative_Docs: ['i am a mathematician with a phd in physics in my s i work as postdoc in a european university and my work involves skills that might be wanted in industry modeling data science coding recently i foresee realistic options to finally obtain a tenure track lecturer position and stabilize my academic career however i keep seeing posts about how shitty academic live is and how much you get paid in industry in comparison i like academia the flexibility the freedom and the work conditions there are of course many issues but i do not think a job in industry would be free of downsides on the other hand i have recently collaborated with some companies and i realize i wouldn t mind working there specially if the salary is significantly higher than at a university i am afraid that the claim industries pay better and academia is shit is from a us point of view and being in a eu country i have no realistic idea whether this still holds i have the feeling universities in europe are less competitive and might offer better conditions including salary do you really think that industry is much better than academia in europe', 'to very briefly summarize and probably overgeneralize national labs if you are interested in academia this is a good path particularly since it exposes you to a different kind of research and working environment you may decide that you like this environment better or at least consider it a good fallback plan since the academic job track is very difficult to break into the national labs tend to be more professional not so much in the sense of starched shirts and business logic but more in the sense of come to work do your job the key is to make sure you work with a supervisor or mentor s who publish frequently as you will need a good publication record to be competitive for academic jobs pay is decent with some of the best fellowships now offering six figures in salary plus you get government level benefits which could be important for you national lab postdocs tend to be considered prestigious some more so than others so the positions may be competitive and difficult to secure industry this is not a good route to go if you want to stay in academia because it is pretty rare for industry researchers to build the kind of publication records that universities look for usually when people make the transition from industry to universities it is after a decently long career building a reputation in their field that they can trade on however the pay in industry is better than anywhere else which is a non trivial consideration academia in a vacuum this would be the best path to academia as you stay close to the arena and ideally learn more about how to work within a university system however in reality the experience varies massively and depends almost entirely on who your supervisor is frankly many supervisors just want you to do research full time at a better quality than their students and don t care about mentoring or your career growth at all if you find the right mentor though it can be beneficial and if your first postdoc experience convinces you that academia isn t what you hoped for it s pretty common at least in my circles to do a second postdoc at a national lab and transition to that arena if it is more to your liking the pay is crap and you will likely suffer financial hardship which imho is the number one reason not to go this route one thing i will add is that the national labs right now are really pushing the ai ml indeed many people at the labs have been pushing this for several years but the management people are very much still seeking explosive growth in these fields that may mean the lab environment is a bit more fertile in your specific case than in general however if you haven t even started a phd program yet this picture may change by the time you are nearing completion so don t consider this a guarantee by any means', 'i worked for several years in ai in both industry and academia in various countries including italy in terms of industry research there is a difference between a research engineer typically someone without a phd and a research scientist position typically someone with a phd while both will typically publish papers the research engineer will have a harder time being able to decide the research agenda which is usually easier for a research scientist overall l also if you want to eventually manage a team in industry a phd will definitely help i used typically a lot because this is not completely deterministic but in general having a phd means you have proven you can do independent research while not having it means you have to do extra work to prove yourself with respect to doing a phd in italy i completely get your concerns in terms of salary i did my phd abroad it s a bit difficult for me to judge your context and constraints but if you re passionate about research i would maybe consider doing a phd elsewhere e g ch or nl have both competitive salaries for phd students you can have this conversation with your advisor i m sure they are painfully aware of the financial aspects of doing academia in italy maybe you can also see if they can help you find a job abroad if you re open to relocating with one of their collaborators for them this could be a good opportunity to continue collaborating with you in any case i would talk to them soon so if you decide to not do a phd they can at least find an alternative candidate']

Name: 89_humans_fear_strawman_argument
Representative_Docs: ['we re just going around in circles because we re coming at it from different angles i think that you make a lot of sense perfect sense if you assume that intelligent behaviour needs to be based on a deep understanding of the logic of the situation that goes beyond the data that they have available you assume that humans have this ability but not ai from that perspective nothing that a llm can do can approach humans because even if it gets to a point that we can t tell the difference between an ai and a human response ai is still ai i d like you to now consider this from the opposite side without considering llms or ai at all what makes you so sure that humans actually act and behave based on logical reasoning and a deep understanding this is counter to everything we know about humans hence my issue that you are placing so much strength of your argument in assuming this as a fact', 'while ai isn t my area of expertise most people would consider me competent enough to discuss it i m training an ai model as we speak i teach a course that has modules on ai development i have published papers that look at the effects of interacting with ai agents the last book i read had several chapters on the ethics of ai if you re in the market for a recommendation check out what we owe the future by william macaskill it has an interesting take on the potential perils of general ai i ve also successfully leveraged ai to create monetized digital products we both seem to agree that ai is going to be a disruptive technology the point of difference is that you seem to see ai as displacing opportunities for humans to generate meaningful thought in contrast i see ai as amplifying the number of opportunities for humans to generate meaningful thought we re allowed to disagree and i don t think either of us is going to change the other s mind i also think we re both making a few too many assumptions so i m not sure this is a debate worth continuing still it was fun enjoy what s left of your weekend and let s both hope that is better than', 'gt ai however intelligent it sounds and appears to be is still only something that has been created and programmed by humans and is therefore not only fallible just like we are but biased as well as someone in the field ai is just fancy math used to make predictions based on examples it s not programmed in the traditional sense but humans can indirectly help decide what it learns by deciding what training data to give it imagine if you had lived under a rock your whole life then joined society and started learning how to spell but you were only being taught by kindergartners that s like bad ai shit data means the ai won t be able to spell any better than a kindergartner but it will be absolutely convinced that it s spelling everything right just because that s what it learned in its view of the world you do spell the english word school as skool anyone who disagrees is incorrect and you d have to retrain the model with better data if you want to change its artificial mind when it comes to biases a lot of humanity is like kindergartners spelling shit wrong but not knowing it that shit seeping into ai obviously isn t ideal but it s also not world ending if we just recognize that the resulting ai is like an artificial clone of our most confidently incorrect blowhards and bigots the real danger comes from people ceding their ground to ai saying well i thought it was spelled school but the ai corrected me guess it s smarter than me and i should fix my spelling no it s not making decisions especially those involving people s livelihoods or even literal lives should not be left up to what amounts to an artificial gullible child']

Representative_Docs: ['i don t know if i need advice or just a mini vent this is far from the greatest frustration any of you face or even i have faced but it just floors me how students still can Name: 91_spellcheck_grammarly_grammar_mechanics writing
Representative_Docs: ['the real answer involves the learning objectives being assessed using spellcheck on a spelling quiz would be cheating using spellcheck on almost any other assignment would be standard practice as the other assignments would likely not be assessing your knowledge of spelling for college level writing assignments grammar is gently not a learning objective so grammarly is fine chatgpt and grammarly go do the research and structure the arguments for you which are the likely learning objectives so is likely cheating', 'i advise my students not to use grammarly spellcheck because it gets in the way of them learning the mechanics of writing the ai generated shit is obviously misconduct but the actual spellchecker is just them shooting themselves in the foot i tell them i don t grade for grammar or all that because i care about their ability to present ideas and complex thought clearly in their writing and sure bad grammar does get in the way of that from time to Name: 92_interview_interviews_rounds_questions
Representative_Docs: ['i m self taught and been learning for about months now i spent about a month leetcoding in december and two weeks in march but that s about it i have a basic grasp of how to solve leetcode easy problems and have a medium understanding of binary trees recursion linked lists i got my first technical take home assessment i wasn t expecting it but now i ve heard back i m determined to do well this would be a great first job it s a small software product studio and they seem to care a lot about strong foundations in dsa i got the technical assessment a few days ago and they are medium medium hard questions one is about cache building i didn t recognize any of the questions from my experience with leetcode panicked and asked for an extension they offered weeks with more time if i ask for it i m tempted to just study the topics to be able to answer the specific questions in the take home but i think that could turn out poorly if they send another assessment after the extension or if they give me another set of questions in the second round interview if i do well on the take home the way i phrased asking for an extension was that i didn t feel comfortable in my preparation of dsa to show my skills and if i could have an extension to better prepare so it was vague and unclear if the extension is for me to prepare for dsa s in general or to work on the specific take home they already sent over i asked chatgpt to generate a syllabus based off of all the dsa s and the types of questions i received in the technical assessment and that was what they recommended study all the basic dsa s in the next days arrays lists hashmap stacks big o string parsing recusion data storage collections spend the days after that focusing on more advanced algorithms and each day doing leetcode problems relevant to the current topic i m definitely nervous about whether i ll understand and feel comfortable with everything by the end of the weeks i could ask for another extension but i don t want to drag out a deadline only to procrastinate i m also wary to ask them if they will be sending me another technical assessment at the end of the weeks or expecting me to have this current one finished by then as i don t want to seem like i m strategizing my approach too much i m also unsure if my current approach is a good one and i should just focus on understanding the dsa s in the questions they gave me wondering if anyone can offer some advice', 'i ve tried making a list of questions to ask during interviews do you think these questions are comprehensible or am i missing some these questions i ll ask for the first interviews why do you work here what s the good and bad about this place what s the culture like which technologies will i use for these categories ui ux design frontend backend database mobile cloud microservices docker kubernetes machine learning other which responsibilities will i have what s the duration of projects how many will i do at once and will it be onsite or at the customer question for consultancy companies what s the onboarding mentorship and learning opportunities on the job like will there be code reviews and pair programming so i can learn from others which other development practices will help me learn from others who will be my manager and team how senior are those people what s the stability and growth of this company like how soon are you expecting the job to start do you have any feedback on my cv do you have any worries about whether i would be successful in this job what s the next step and what s the whole interview process like these questions i ll ask when getting the job offer what s the compensation like salary overtime pay bonus stock retirement contribution which benefits are there insurances pto food computer telephone internet other will i be able to work remotely some days of the week can i work from other countries i ll pretend that i ask this because my mom advised to ask about this in job interviews to gauge the flexibility of the company and not because i need it are unpaid sabbaticals possible after how many months and how long can they be again i ll pretend that i ask this because my mom advised to ask about this in job interviews to gauge the flexibility of the company and not because i need it which advancements are possible in this position what are the work hours like what s the flexibility of when i do these hours is overtime common is lunch counted as work hours how is performance measured these things i ll try to research on my own company size company reputation how would i research this asking former employees what they think of the company reading glassdoor reviews amp x b what else could i ask and do to chose between job offers', 'i was laid off earlier in the summer and wanted to share my experience getting a job in the current market i m a senior software engineer with around years of experience working on backend systems after the layoff i took a month off to go on vacation and then spent a few weeks preparing for interviews once i felt i was ready i scheduled a bunch of interviews and went through all the processes here is the sankey diagram of how it went applications the majority of my applications were through referrals though i did a few cold applications as well once i announced my layoff many of my former managers leads and coworkers reached out to offer referrals i also personally reached out to friends and former coworkers for referrals as well i was picky with which companies and projects i want for my next role so i kept my pool relatively small interview process the interview process seems unchanged from the last time i interviewed which was in its usually a chat with the hm technical screen followed by the onsite all of this was remote via meets zoom and shared web coding ide onsite structure the onsite mostly consisted of coding rounds system design round leadership behavioral round some companies would swap a coding round for an extra system design round coding rounds as a senior se there s less coding rounds than junior levels but the questions are usually more difficult some examples of questions i got are below these aren t exact of the questions i got but similar enough in concepts overall not too difficult system design rounds these were very standard system design rounds the interviewers want to dive deep on a specific topic part of the system so the method is to lay out your high level design as fast as you can and constantly communicate with the interviewers to figure out which portion to dive deep on for me a combination of working on massively distributed backend systems and some studying was enough to pass these rounds behavioral and leadership rounds these are basically just a chat about my past experiences challenges i ve faced impact i ve made mentorship i have provided experience leading projects etc i didn t really do any prep for these as i m pretty good at just bragging about myself and my accomplishments thoughts on the market overall the market is much cooler than but it s not a bad market right now there s still many companies hiring for senior and staff se roles a lot of faang isn t hiring right now and meta opened a lot of positions but reached out to me too late for me to consider them but there s a lot of big tech companies outside of faang for example stripe block atlassian coinbase affirm dropbox snap etc are all hiring right now offers my goal was mostly to get on an interesting project and at the very least maintain the comp i was making the offer i accepted was a senior software engineer role with around a bump in tc which isn t bad considering my goal was to just break even i didn t really see the trend of layoffs suppressing wages as is touted on this sub as all of the offers i got were better but not by a lot than what i was making it took me around weeks to secure my offers not counting my vacation i start in mid august as i want to get some more traveling done this summer final thoughts overall not a bad experience i didn t even run out of severance before securing a offer given all the doom and gloom and the ai articles online you would think that the market would be worse but turns out if you re a strong engineer with a history of driving impact are social and made connections at work and practiced interviewing enough there shouldn t be many issues in weathering a layoff feel free to ask any questions i ll try to respond with advice or details as i can']

Name: 95_disagree_pants_education_agree
Representative_Docs: ['your first line is easy to say but very hard to agree on how to operationalize for instance you might feel that any written feedback that should a student take it will improve a student s writing has been a job done well in this case you ll have thought that education is fundamentally about skilling about depositing well defined capacities into human bodies so to speak many people would agree with you unfortunately even more would regard this as good enough which is the real dilemma generative ai presents that more paymasters in more contexts will regard machine produced products as good enough relative to ultimate marketplaces which after all can always be persuaded to bear lower quality goods and services at the right price point what actually gets dramatized by the ai dilemma is how fundamentally not objective the magic of the price mechanism is in determining social value someone like me and many students themselves if given both the option and the means to understand it would disagree believing that education should be about the formation of persons as such we would see computer feedback on student writing as not done well intrinsically any time it interfered with the ongoing human conversation between an expert and a learner that fosters the learner s development in stochastic but patterned ways people who hold this latter view would say that this human development not skilling is the essence of education as a social good not only because it leads to a more meaningful life for the individual but because it produces a better society on the whole even as it is of course necessary that skills and specific pieces of knowledge be part of that very development now regardless of which of us is right in some final sense what this demonstrates is that your initial statement is just not dispositive determining which things a computer program can do well fundamentally depends especially in the context of education on what a person believes a good society is and how one may be formed obviously you understand that since it s the thrust of your second paragraph not just in the extreme though basally so so i imagine you ll agree with me that the first line is a bit of a throwaway ultimately just a way of dodging the question', 'pants are a good example actually and your acknowledgment that a whole book could be written on the tradeoff gives the lie to your certainty that where we ve ended up is better because it would be possible to write a whole book indeed people have in part on the massive environmental damage of fast fashion and more broadly high plastic high waste consumption in other words the externalities and deferred costs of that one society level decision point have turned out in part as these costs are only now becoming clear to be massively for the worse indeed even without getting into all that from a sheerly economic standpoint the tradeoff s much less clear than you d like it to be if a dollar pair of pants is good for a year or two of washing the dollar pair maybe three or four and the dollar pair ten or twenty incidentally i base those numbers on the fact that i almost exclusively buy tag on new used pants at thrift stores myself i happen to have a pretty good sense of lifecycle on this point and moreover the economy organized around the dollar pair of pants is one in which the person who buys those can also own a home and retire as against the current world which puts both these out of touch for the majority i think a person would be very hard pressed to say that the society of the dollar pants was a better deal for the majority of pants buyers at any rate and this is the real point here it is indeed far from being an obvious tradeoff there the same is very much and to my mind very obviously true for generative ai the notion that we should all bow to a whatever the worst the market will bear mentality for education is indeed though already in place for many people as you note in my view quite clearly insane i mean that in a strong clinical sense as in there are a series of observable realities that have been clear negative sequelae of such a view up to and including the radical degradation of our planet s carrying capacity for our species and nearly all others which as deferred costs by definition cannot in fact be deferred forever such that holding still to the basal view of markets as magic is strictly speaking incompatible with competent sense perception of the world around us and species general future planning i think given what we know about how negative externalities and deferred costs both can be modeled for and habitually are not anyone who values the understanding of education i laid out above would be a fool to just start outsourcing their labor to ai willy nilly it s something to be navigated thoughtfully not automatically condemned but even more important not automatically embraced across all dimensions of the work', 'on the chronicle published an article that contains the thoughts on ai of figures in higher ed in the linked piece i highlight claims of the first that i find plausible implausible and less certain and i explain why i agree or disagree i think the chronicle article deserves more attention and discussion amongst professors here are the bitesized highlights from my responses i agree with bryan caplan that there will be a delay before ai reaches its full potential in changing the labor market and students career aspirations but i discuss two big caveats tech disruptions are gaining speed ai is already disrupting i disagree with bryan caplan that ai makes unmonitored academic work optional or a farce why it is feasible to design one s course to incentivize students to do unmonitored work at home as a condition on successfully completing in class activities and assessments contra bryan caplan i argue that it is uncertain that there will be no major disruption for higher education from ai it is not certain that students will enroll in majors at the same rates that they currently do many fields will need to incorporate new ai training i agree with jeffrey j selingo that ai tools will become central to some admissions and recruiting tasks they can help with e g vetting undergraduates applications for specific characteristics i disagree with jeffrey j selingo that the immediate impact of generative ai will likely be felt outside of the academic core where the academic core is defined as teaching learning and research it immediately affected teaching and learning via plagiarism contra jeffrey j selingo i argue that it is uncertain that only if universities use ai to take over for people in jobs that involve processing information or doing repeatable tasks that we will reverse or slow down the upward cost trajectory of higher education productivity and its effects are unpredictable and there are other pathways to cost reduction i agree with philip kitcher that in the long er run much work will be taken over by ai and jobs that can be completed by ai tools will be completed by them rather than humans this is to assume that kitcher is not referring to jobs vanishing in general i disagree with philip kitcher that higher education does not and should not also aim to train students to work in addition to develop them as individuals and as citizens work is and ought to be an important and rewarding part of many people s lives and many students rightly see higher education as primarily part of their career development especially generation z i agree with hollis robbins that in the era of ai specific and local cultural knowledge will become more valuable and experiential learning will become the norm on the first our investigation into the ai immunity of take home assignments has already shown that generative ai tools struggle to generate responses as the assessments field specific standards increase i disagree with hollis robbins that higher education will be about ensuring students read what is not yet known by ai why most of what undergraduates read is known by ai already most of what undergraduates should read is known by ai already just as much of the mathematical techniques and methods that students should learn are known by calculators wolframalpha etc contra hollis robbins it is uncertain that the written essay will no longer be the default for student assessment because there is still value to having students write essays in class and there is still value to having students write essays with ai assistance i agree with ted underwood that students will need the skills and knowledge to evaluate the responses of ai tools and which ai tools to use in the first place even if they will use ai tools heavily in their jobs i disagree with ted underwood that chatgpt and other chatbots should be conceptualized as tools where we ask a short question and get a short answer why prompt sizes and modalities are expanding integrations contra ted underwood it is uncertain that llms will remain engines for provisional reasoning we have already seen radical increases in llm accuracy and effectiveness in a short period of time for instance gpt significantly outperforms gpt on a range of tests i agree with g gabrielle starr that active learning synthesis and application will increase in importance as ai tools become ubiquitous i disagree with g gabrielle starr that the pleasures of learning differentiate humans from ai with respect to creativity and active experimentation some ai tools are very creative especially when left to their own devices i also disagree with g gabrielle starr that direct influences on the world around us is what should differentiate humans from ai ai is already directly influencing innumerable aspects of our world and has been for years contra g gabrielle starr it is uncertain that chatgpt and other generative ai tools will not take away the pleasures of learning there is some evidence that people prefer completing some tasks with ai tools than without nevertheless it is not at all clear that there will not be some pleasures of learning that will be reduced by these tools what do you all think']

Representative_Docs: ['i tell my students that i expect that within years anybody writing reports will either be using generative ai or doing their job wrong we re pretty much just waiting on the grant without the use of some form of generative ai again', 'i honestly wonder if generative ai might wind up increasing some of the need for fact based learning using these systems requires a fair amount of specific knowledge at the very least specific vocabulary and being able to evaluate the results might require a fair amount of foundational knowledge one example when i teach poetry i don t spend a lot of time on rhyme scheme and almost zero on meter for the most part it doesn t matter students can intuit rhyme schemes to construct their own poetry without necessarily knowing how to break it down when they see it the different types of meters are also not particularly relevant to our goals students don t need to be able to label anapaestic or iambic for any of my outcomes but to use generative ai to construct poetry you need to be able to label these things if you ask it to generate a shakespearean sonnet or a limerick it ll give you a poem that kind of resembles a or line poem to get the result you re looking for you have to start using more specific terminology produce a line shakespearean sonnet about mcdonald s big macs using the ababcdcdefefgg rhyme scheme and written in iambic tetrameter that s a pretty narrow application mind you but the need for precision language and instruction to use the program and evaluate the results is probably greater than you need to generate results on your own the ai saves time and labor but requires a degree of accuracy beyond what we need when communicating with or to humans', 'hello there i understand your skepticism about the prevalence of students using generative ai in their assignments while it s true that we cannot definitively prove each instance of ai usage it s important to consider the evidence and potential implications as educators we often develop a keen sense of our students capabilities and writing styles over time when we encounter sudden and drastic improvements in their work it raises suspicions that something beyond their usual abilities is at play while this doesn t confirm the use of ai it prompts us to investigate further and ensure academic integrity moreover the advancements in generative ai technology have made it increasingly accessible and user friendly students themselves often discuss and share information about these tools making it reasonable to assume that some of them may be leveraging ai to assist in their assignments rather than dismissing the concerns as a fad we should encourage open conversations and awareness about the potential challenges associated with generative ai by addressing these issues we can develop effective strategies to uphold academic integrity and maintain a fair learning environment for all students it s essential to emphasize that our goal is not to point fingers or accuse students indiscriminately instead we should focus on equipping ourselves with knowledge and tools to detect and prevent academic dishonesty effectively by being vigilant and having discussions like this we can ensure the authenticity and value of the education we provide to our students you can spot this shit from a mile off if you have even a bit of experience reading and evaluating student writing']

the learning process and intrinsic motivation', 'here it is it is pretty pro ai q draft a college course policy on using ai chatgpt course policy on using artificial intelligence ai purpose the purpose of this course policy is to outline the guidelines and expectations for the responsible and ethical use of artificial intelligence ai technologies within college courses this policy aims to foster a safe inclusive and effective learning environment while promoting the responsible application of ai in academic settings scope this policy applies to all college courses that involve the use of ai technologies or incorporate ai related assignments projects or research responsible ai use all ai related activities within the course must adhere to the principles of responsible ai use this includes but is not limited to ensuring fairness transparency accountability and privacy when designing implementing and deploying ai systems ethical considerations students and instructors are expected to address ethical considerations related to ai use this includes discussing potential biases in ai algorithms the implications of ai on privacy and security and the potential impact of ai technologies on society particularly marginalized communities academic integrity students are required to uphold academic integrity when using ai technologies in assignments projects or research plagiarism unauthorized collaboration or using ai for dishonest purposes are strictly prohibited and may result in disciplinary actions as per the college s academic integrity policies data privacy and consent when working with data for ai related activities students and instructors must prioritize data privacy and obtain appropriate consent from individuals or entities whose data is used sensitive and personally identifiable information should be handled with utmost care ensuring compliance with relevant laws and regulations inclusivity and accessibility ai technologies should be designed and implemented with inclusivity and accessibility in mind efforts must be made to ensure that ai applications do not discriminate against individuals based on factors such as race gender religion disability or other protected characteristics transparency and explainability ai algorithms and models used in the course should be transparent and explainable to the extent possible students should understand how ai decisions are made and be able to explain the rationale behind the results human oversight while ai can be a valuable tool it is essential to acknowledge its limitations human oversight and critical thinking must remain an integral part of the decision making process when using ai technologies collaboration and support collaboration among students in ai related coursework is encouraged as long as it adheres to the collaboration guidelines set forth by the instructor students should also have access to resources and support to understand and effectively use ai technologies emerging technologies and updates as ai is a rapidly evolving field the course content and policies may be subject to updates to reflect the latest developments and best practices students and instructors should stay informed about the latest advancements and ethical considerations in ai consequences of policy violation any violations of this policy will be addressed according to the college s academic integrity policies and may lead to appropriate consequences which could include but are not limited to failing grades course expulsion or other disciplinary actions this policy is subject to review and update as needed instructors are encouraged to incorporate discussions about ai ethics responsibility and policy considerations into their teaching to ensure students are well prepared for the ethical challenges in the ai driven world']
