Name: 118_turnitin_ai detectors_detectors_false positives
Representative_Docs: ['in this evolving climate of ai ai detectors and ai bypassers it feels as though it s becoming an arms race especially with turnitin being used almost universally so the question when marking an assignment do you use the ai detector if so what percentage makes you jump the gun i m intrigued as i am noticing an uproar in false positive claims and a lot are unsure how to proceed', 'i m a split time instructor and course designer at a big state school recently turnitin released their ai detection feature and i m not particularly impressed mostly it s given me false positives but it s caught a few students my biggest concern though is i think i m noticing a pattern in the false positives most of the students either have a reading learning disability or are ell i ve only got students this term so hey that isn t a huge sample but i just wanted to see if anyone else on here has noticed similar trends or has any other thoughts about the tii ai detection thanks for your time and hope your semester ends well in grading hell myself right now', 'hi everyone in the run down to the end of the year ai detection has become a necessity we will need a robust ai detection system in place before the next school year begins in my school we are encouraging a robust set of processes and behavioural procedures in how students develop their work so we have development steps on record for comparison against final products plus the use of ai detectors we have turnitin and teachers are also taking the initiative and using a whole range of the new ai detectors out there but which of them are reliable and perhaps more importantly how do we defend the result of one detector versus another for one student task i have what appear to be four false positives from turnitin for three of the tasks the other detectors all identified different passages at different levels but all with very low percentages below but the fourth one it reported one student s work at ai generated i then re checked the paper for confirmation at winston ai zerogpt gptzero copyleaks writer and grammarly what gives we ve now had several confrontations between students teachers and then parents due to false positives in a few instances the student has admitted to using it but the online communities all tell students to just deny deny deny and we can t prove anything and based on these results ranging from yeah it really seems we can t prove anything so what ai detectors are you using']

Name: 119_professor_wrote_detection_history
Representative_Docs: ['totally agree with this i had to do something similar when a professor questioned my paper s originality even though i wrote every word myself it s wild how these ai detectors can throw false flags and leave you scrambling for proof what helped me a ton was keeping all my notes screenshots of doc history and even timestamps from my drafts that experience was such a mess that i started using koalaessays afterward not to cheat but because i wanted a solid structure to build off of you still get to write your own stuff but their research help makes it way easier to defend your work this review breaks it down pretty well medium com suvorovgerasim best writing service my honest review of koalaessays a a c f having a clean cited and organized starting point saved me from another turnitin panic just in case things go south again it s worth having that kind of backup', 'if i were you i would schedule a meeting and discuss this with the professor in person if you have your edit work history of your assignment available that could also help i don t think your professor is following good practice because ai detection is not very good it s well known to not be very good and is pretty low if your professor is not willing to discuss this with you then it would be worth going to the department chair and ask for clarification regarding the use of ai detection and what kind of safeguards there are against false positives well known historical texts have been flagged as being written by ai which is obviously ridiculous', 'don t change your writing style to avoid getting flagged by an ai detector instead use a writing program like google docs that tracks edit history or save multiple versions using save as with different timestamps if using word if a detectors but if you truly did your own work you can defeat the accusation with evidence of your work process and knowledge']

we have seen is due to this ai reviewer tool this tool has been so effective that we gained enough confidence to allow our devs to merge prs after they resolve all the ai comments and ping other developers only when they need a second opinion its unique features are line by line code suggestions reviews the changes line by line and provides code change suggestions that can be directly in the context of lines of code or entire files helpful in providing context generating test cases and reducing code complexity smart review skipping by default skips in depth review for simple Representative_Docs: ['yeah after waffling on allowing ai use in my classes last spring and summer i m drawing a hard line this fall and forbidding it our academic integrity team is finally starting to encourage that too whereas last year our writing center was holding a bunch of feel good b s seminars on how to use it on purpose no thanks', 'be careful with this line of thought images usually create accessibility problems unless they have appropriate alt text which would defeat the purpose of using images also the tactic can also be defeated using simple ocr i just took a screenshot of your comment and uploaded it to newocr com which produced trailmix pprof hr ago along those lines also use images in the text as a point of reference for questions a u sy e reply share consequently these ways of trying to prevent use of ai are really just requiring more work to make content less accessible only to make it slightly less convenient to use ai same goes for audio only instructions speech to text is easy to use and fairly accurate if you want to curb students use of ai as a crutch you need to make it explicitly forbidden to use and pray you have a way to actually accurately detect and prove its use or design novel problems that can t be answered well by ai and or allow its use in appropriate ways and demonstrate how to take advantage of ai to aide learning not to replace learning', 'in my last year of my bachelor s in forensics when it first came out or got popular i played around with it like you do siri asked stupid questions jokes dinner ideas etc if and how professors have not been using ai for any reason mostly because there s no reason to use it they ve been using the same powerpoint for the last three to four years or they re premade from pearson also is it common for people to use chatgpt now on homework exams etc personally i ve never used it nor have i ever seen someone explicitly use it for school work so far it s only been by rumors but original work that s if they don t just give you a full zero most tests exams have become honor locked or in person because of this very reason and that flashcard site i ve mostly heard it being used
